title: Sunset Boat Trip Marbella — Cava & Skipper Included
metaDescription: Book a sunset boat trip Marbella with cava and skipper included. Cruise the Costa del Sol coast from Puerto Banús. Check availability and prices at BoatHire24.
h1: Sunset Boat Trip Marbella, Cava & Skipper Included
intro:
You want a sunset boat trip Marbella that actually delivers: a cold glass of cava, a competent skipper who knows the best spots, and no hidden fees. The good news is that every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper and a bottle of cava on board, so you can just turn up and enjoy the show. Departures run from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena, with trips typically lasting two to three hours as the sun drops behind La Concha mountain.

Whether you are after a private sunset cruise for two or a party with friends on a catamaran, the process is simple: pick your date, choose your boat, and confirm. No negotiating, no surprise charges. The skipper handles the navigation, the anchor, and the music if you want it. You just sit back, sip the cava, and watch the sky turn orange over the Mediterranean.

Below you will find exactly what is included, which ports work best for sunset, what boat types suit different groups, and how to book without the usual hassle. This is the straight-talking guide to sunset boat trips in Marbella, written by people who actually run them.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

Every sunset boat trip Marbella booked through BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper who holds a Spanish navigation licence. You do not need a licence yourself, even if you want to take the helm for a photo. The skipper will handle mooring, anchoring, and safety briefings. Also included is a bottle of cava (Spanish sparkling wine) per booking, not per person. If you want more drinks, you can bring your own or order extras through the skipper at cost. Ice, cups, and a cooler are provided. Fuel is included in the price for the standard trip duration. If you want to extend the trip or go further along the coast, additional fuel costs may apply, but the skipper will quote that upfront.

Life jackets, a first-aid kit, and basic safety equipment are mandatory and provided. Most boats also have a Bluetooth speaker, a bimini top for shade, and a swim ladder if you fancy a dip during the golden hour. The exact amenities depend on the boat you choose, but the listing on the site will show photos and a full equipment list. No hidden extras. What you see is what you get.

Best Departure Ports for a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

Puerto Banús is the most popular departure point for a sunset boat trip Marbella, and for good reason. You leave the marina, pass the superyachts, and within 15 minutes you are anchored off the coast with a direct view of the sun sinking behind the Sierra Blanca mountains. The water is calm, the breeze is light, and the sky does the work. From Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), the trip is slightly shorter to reach open water, and you get a great view of the Marbella coastline lit up as dusk falls. Cabopino is quieter, with fewer boats around, which suits couples or small groups who want a more private experience. Estepona offers a more rugged coastline, with the possibility of seeing the sun set over the Strait of Gibraltar on clear days. Sotogrande is further west and works well if you are staying on that side of the coast. Benalmádena is a solid option if you are based near Fuengirola or Málaga, though the journey to the sunset spot is a bit longer.

Each port has its own character. Puerto Banús is flashy, loud, and full of energy. Marbella Marina is more relaxed but still central. Cabopino is sleepy and underrated. Estepona is authentic and less touristy. Pick the port that matches your vibe. The skipper will know the best anchorage for sunset based on the wind and swell that day, so trust their judgment.

Boat Types for a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

You can book a motor yacht, a catamaran, a sailing yacht, a RIB, or a licence-free day boat for your sunset boat trip Marbella. Motor yachts are the most common choice: they are fast, stable, and have plenty of deck space for lounging. A typical 40-foot motor yacht can take up to 10 guests comfortably. Catamarans are wider and more stable, ideal if someone in your group gets seasick. They also have a large trampoline net at the front where you can lie down and watch the sunset. Sailing yachts offer a quieter, more traditional experience, but they are slower and less spacious. RIBs are fast and fun, but they are not great for lounging, they are better for a quick sunset blast along the coast with a stop for a swim. Licence-free day boats are small, simple, and cheap, but they are not ideal for sunset trips because they lack the power and comfort for a proper cruise. Superyachts are available on request, but expect prices from around EUR 5,000 for a two-hour sunset charter.

For a group of 6 to 10 people, a motor yacht is the sweet spot. For a couple or a small family, a sailing yacht or a RIB can work, but check the photos carefully, some RIBs have no toilet or shade. For a party, a catamaran with a large deck and a sound system is the way to go. Browse the options on the search page and filter by boat type, capacity, and port.

Pricing Guidance for a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

Prices for a sunset boat trip Marbella vary depending on the boat size, the port, the season, and the duration. A typical two-hour sunset charter on a 35-foot motor yacht from Puerto Banús starts from around EUR 350 for up to 8 guests. A larger 50-foot motor yacht for 10 guests will cost from around EUR 600. Catamarans are slightly more expensive due to their size and stability: from around EUR 450 for a 40-foot cat. Sailing yachts are cheaper, from around EUR 250 for a 30-foot boat, but they are slower and you may not cover as much coastline. RIBs start from around EUR 200 for a two-hour trip. Licence-free day boats are the cheapest, from around EUR 150, but they are not recommended for sunset trips because they have no toilet and limited seating.

High season (June to September) sees prices roughly 20-30% higher than low season (October to May). Weekends are also more expensive than weekdays. If you are flexible on the date, you can save money by booking a Monday to Thursday trip. The price includes the skipper, cava, fuel, and basic amenities. Gratuity for the skipper is not included but is customary at around 10-15%. You can tip in cash at the end of the trip. Always confirm the exact total before booking. The site shows the final price including VAT, so there are no surprises.

Best Season for a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

The best season for a sunset boat trip Marbella is from May to October, when the weather is warm and the sun sets late. In May, the temperature is around 22 degrees C at sunset, and the water is still cool but swimmable. June to August is peak season: air temperature around 28-32 degrees C, water temperature 24-26 degrees C, and the sun sets around 21:30 to 21:45. September is still excellent, with similar temperatures but fewer crowds. October is the shoulder month: still pleasant at around 22 degrees C, but the sun sets earlier, around 19:30. November to April is possible but less comfortable: temperatures drop to 15-18 degrees C, the wind picks up, and the sun sets early. Some boats still operate in winter, but you will need a jacket, and the experience is not the same. Most skippers recommend booking between May and September for the best sunset experience.

The sunset time changes by about two minutes per day, so check the exact time for your date. The skipper will typically suggest a departure time 30 to 45 minutes before sunset, so you have time to get to the anchorage, settle in, and watch the full show. The trip usually ends about 30 minutes after sunset, giving you time to see the afterglow and the first stars.

How to Book a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

Booking a sunset boat trip Marbella on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the site, enter your preferred date and port, and browse the available boats. Each listing shows the boat capacity, price, amenities, and a photo gallery. Click on a boat to see the full description and the skipper's profile. Once you find one you like, select the date and time, and complete the booking with a deposit (typically 30-50% of the total). The remaining balance is paid on the day of the trip, either in cash or by card, depending on the skipper's preference. You will receive a confirmation email with the meeting point details and the skipper's contact number.

If you are unsure which boat to choose, use the search page to filter by capacity, price range, and boat type. You can also read the FAQ section below for common questions. If you need help, contact the support team via the chat on the site. They are based in Marbella and know the boats and skippers personally. They can recommend a boat based on your group size and budget. Do not leave booking until the last minute, especially in July and August, when popular boats sell out days in advance. Book at least a week ahead for the best availability.

Who Is a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella For

A sunset boat trip Marbella is for anyone who wants a memorable evening on the water without the stress of planning. Couples book it for anniversaries, proposals, or just a romantic date. Groups of friends book it for birthdays, hen parties, or a night out with a difference. Families book it for a relaxed end to a holiday. Business clients book it for corporate entertaining or team-building. The trip is suitable for all ages, but children under 12 should wear a life jacket at all times, and the skipper will brief you on safety. If you have mobility issues, check the boat's accessibility before booking, some boats have steep steps or no wheelchair access. The skipper can usually accommodate special requests like a specific playlist, a vegetarian platter, or a later return time, but ask at the time of booking.

What you will not get is a rushed, impersonal experience. The skipper is there for you, not for a crowd. You can decide the pace, the music, and the route within reason. If you want to stop for a swim, say so. If you want to stay longer at the anchorage, ask. The flexibility is part of the appeal. For more ideas on what to do along the coast, check the blog for guides on Marbella's best beaches, restaurants, and secret coves.

What to Bring on a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

Bring sunscreen, even for a sunset trip, the sun is still strong in the late afternoon. A light jacket or sweater is useful because the wind picks up after sunset. Sunglasses and a hat are essential. If you plan to swim, bring a towel and a change of clothes. The boat has a small toilet on most motor yachts and catamarans, but not on RIBs or small day boats. Check the listing for toilet availability. You can bring your own food and drinks, but glass bottles are not allowed on some boats for safety reasons. Use plastic cups or cans. The skipper will provide a cooler with ice for your drinks. Do not bring anything valuable that could get wet or blown overboard. Cameras and phones are fine, but keep them in a waterproof case. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill an hour before departure. The water is usually calm in the evening, but it can be choppy if the wind picks up.

The skipper will have a basic first-aid kit, but bring any personal medication you need. If you have a medical condition, inform the skipper before departure. The boat is not a party bus, you cannot bring loudspeakers or generators. Keep the music at a reasonable volume out of respect for other boats and the environment. The skipper has the right to refuse boarding if you are intoxicated or aggressive. Be sensible, and everyone will have a good time.

Local Colour and Sights on a Sunset Boat Trip Marbella

During a sunset boat trip Marbella, you will see the coastline from a different perspective. The golden hour light hits the whitewashed buildings of Puerto Banús and the hills behind. If you are lucky, you might spot dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar, especially on trips from Estepona or Sotogrande. The skipper can point out landmarks like La Concha mountain, which dominates the skyline, or the Río Verde estuary, where you sometimes see herons and egrets. Cala del Faro is a small cove near Marbella that is popular for anchoring during sunset, it is sheltered and has a rocky cliff that glows orange in the evening light. Further east, the Nerja caves are too far for a standard sunset trip, but you can combine a day trip with a sunset charter if you book a full-day boat.

Estepona old town is visible from the water, with its colourful flower pots and narrow streets. The skipper can moor close to the shore so you get a good view. The Strait of Gibraltar is a migration route for birds and marine life, so keep your eyes open for shearwaters, gulls, and the occasional whale. The sunset itself is the main event, and the best spots are usually just off the coast, away from the marina lights, where the sky turns from blue to pink to deep purple. The skipper will choose the anchorage based on the wind and the position of the sun. Trust them, they have done this hundreds of times.

Safety and Legal Requirements

All skippers on BoatHire24 hold a valid Spanish navigation licence and have passed a background check. The boats are insured and inspected regularly. The skipper will give a safety briefing before departure, covering life jacket locations, emergency procedures, and the location of the first-aid kit. Life jackets are mandatory for children under 12 and for anyone who cannot swim. They are available for all passengers but not required for adults unless the skipper deems it necessary due to weather conditions. The boat has a VHF radio and a flare kit for emergencies. The skipper is trained in basic first aid and CPR. In the unlikely event of an accident, the skipper will contact the coastguard (channel 16 on VHF) and follow their instructions.

You must be at least 18 years old to book, but children are welcome. The boat has a maximum capacity set by the manufacturer and the insurance policy. Do not exceed this number. The skipper has the right to refuse boarding if the number of guests exceeds the limit or if anyone is visibly intoxicated. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper will not serve anyone who is drunk. The boat is not a bar. You are responsible for your own behaviour. If you damage the boat, you may be liable for the repair cost. Most skippers ask for a damage deposit of around EUR 200 to EUR 500, which is refunded after the trip if there is no damage. Check the booking terms for the exact amount.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Yacht Charter Puerto Banús, Day & Sunset Hire
  • Boat Rental Marbella — Skipper Included from Puerto Banús
  • Catamaran Charter Marbella — Half & Full Day Cruises

faqs.q: What time does a sunset boat trip Marbella start?
faqs.a: The skipper will suggest a departure time 30 to 45 minutes before sunset, so you have time to reach the anchorage and settle in. The exact time depends on the season. In summer, sunset is around 21:30, so you might leave at 20:45. In spring or autumn, sunset is earlier, around 19:30, so departure is around 18:45. You will receive the exact time in your confirmation email.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on a sunset boat trip Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and non-alcoholic drinks. The boat provides a cooler with ice. Glass bottles are not allowed on some boats for safety reasons, so use plastic or cans. The cava is included, but you can bring extra bottles of wine or beer if you want. The skipper will not charge corkage, but you must drink responsibly.

faqs.q: How many people can join a sunset boat trip Marbella?
faqs.a: The capacity depends on the boat. A typical motor yacht takes up to 10 guests. Catamarans can take up to 12. RIBs and sailing yachts take 6 to 8. The boat listing shows the maximum number of passengers. Do not exceed this limit, as it is a safety requirement. Children count as passengers. The skipper will check the number on boarding.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my sunset boat trip Marbella?
faqs.a: If the weather is unsafe (high winds, thunderstorms, or rough seas), the skipper will cancel the trip and you will receive a full refund or the option to reschedule. The skipper makes the call based on the forecast and conditions. In most cases, evening trips are calm, but summer storms can occur. You will be notified at least two hours before departure if the trip is cancelled.

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence for a sunset boat trip Marbella?
faqs.a: No, you do not need a licence. Every boat includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. You can take the helm for a photo if you want, but the skipper remains in control at all times. This applies to all boat types, including RIBs and day boats. Just turn up and enjoy.

faqs.q: Can I book a sunset boat trip Marbella for a proposal or special occasion?
faqs.a: Yes, many people do. The skipper can help arrange a special moment, such as playing a specific song, anchoring in a private spot, or coordinating with a photographer on shore. You should mention the occasion when booking so the skipper can prepare. There is no extra charge, but a tip for the skipper is appreciated if they go out of their way.

faqs.q: Is a sunset boat trip Marbella suitable for children?
faqs.a: Yes, children are welcome. Life jackets are provided and must be worn by children under 12. The boat is a safe environment, but parents should supervise at all times. The trip is calm and relaxed, so it is suitable for most ages. If your child is prone to seasickness, bring medication. The skipper can also adjust the route to avoid rough water.

title: Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella — Superyachts & Crew
metaDescription: Book a luxury yacht rental Marbella with a skipper. Superyachts, catamarans, motor yachts from Puerto Banús. Instant online booking, no hidden fees.
h1: Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella, Superyachts & Crew
intro:
You want a luxury yacht rental in Marbella, and you want it done right. No messing about with owners who don't answer, no hidden fees, and definitely no boats that look better in the photos than they do on the water. BoatHire24 is a marketplace that lists only verified boats with licensed skippers, based in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina. You pick the boat, we handle the rest.

Whether it's a 20-metre motor yacht for a birthday bash or a 50-metre superyacht for a corporate event, the process is the same: you see the real availability, you book online, and you turn up on the day. The skipper is included in every charter, so you don't need a licence or any experience. Just bring your sunglasses and your wallet.
bodyHtml:
What You Get With a Luxury Yacht Rental in Marbella

A luxury yacht rental in Marbella means more than just a boat. It means a crew that knows the coast, a boat that's been cleaned and checked, and an itinerary that makes sense. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper and fuel for a standard day (usually 8 hours, 10:00 to 18:00). You also get a safety briefing, life jackets, and a basic sound system. Some boats throw in paddleboards, snorkel gear, or a cooler with ice. Check the listing details before you book.

What you don't get is a captain who doubles as a waiter or a host who also drives the boat. The skipper drives and handles safety. If you want catering, bar service, or a DJ, you can add those as extras. Most operators offer a catering upgrade from around EUR 35 per person for a basic lunch, or EUR 75 for a full menu with drinks. You can also bring your own food and drink on most boats, but check the policy first. Some owners charge a corkage fee for outside alcohol.

Fuel is included for up to 60 nautical miles of cruising. If you want to go further, say to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins, you pay the difference. That's usually EUR 200 to EUR 500 extra depending on the boat and distance.

Puerto Banús: The Epicentre of Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

Puerto Banús is where most luxury yacht rentals in Marbella start. It's the marina with the superyachts, the designer shops, and the restaurants where you might spot a footballer. The port has 915 berths, and boats up to 50 metres can dock here. If you're renting a superyacht, it will almost certainly depart from Banús.

The marina is about 15 minutes from Marbella town centre by taxi. There's paid parking nearby, but it fills up fast in summer. If you're staying in a hotel in the Golden Mile or Sierra Blanca, it's a short drive. The port has a few cafes and a supermarket if you need to stock up before you board.

From Puerto Banús, a typical luxury yacht charter heads west towards Cabopino or east towards Marbella Marina and Cala del Faro. You can anchor for a swim, have lunch at a chiringuito, or just cruise along the coast. The water is calm most of the year, with average sea temperatures of 22 degrees C in summer and 16 in winter.

Other Departure Ports for Your Marbella Yacht Charter

While Puerto Banús is the main hub, you can also start a luxury yacht rental Marbella charter from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Each port has its own character.

Marbella Marina is smaller and less flashy than Banús, but it's closer to the old town and the beach clubs. Cabopino is a quiet marina near the Dunas de Artola nature reserve, good for a relaxed day out. Estepona has a pretty old town and a marina that's less crowded. Sotogrande is a bit further west, near the Sotogrande polo fields and golf courses. Benalmádena is east of Marbella, close to Fuengirola and the Selwo Marina park.

If you're staying in a villa near Sotogrande, it makes sense to depart from there. If you're in a hotel on the Marbella beachfront, Marbella Marina is more convenient. The choice is yours, but Banús is the default for most luxury charters.

Types of Boats Available for Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

BoatHire24 lists several types of boats for luxury yacht rental in Marbella. Here's what you can expect.

Motor yachts are the most common. They range from 10 metres to 30 metres, with cabins below deck and a flybridge for sunbathing. A typical 15-metre motor yacht sleeps 6 to 8 guests and costs from around EUR 1,500 per day in low season, EUR 2,500 in high season. For a 20-metre, expect EUR 3,000 to EUR 5,000.

Catamarans are wider and more stable, with a shallow draft. They're good for groups who want space and a smooth ride. A 12-metre catamaran costs from around EUR 1,200 per day. They have a large trampoline at the front and usually a flybridge with a dining area.

Sailing yachts are less common for luxury charters, but they're available. A 14-metre sailing yacht costs from around EUR 900 per day. The skipper handles the sails, so you just relax.

RIBs and speedboats are for smaller groups who want speed. A 9-metre RIB with twin outboards can hit 40 knots. Costs from around EUR 600 for half a day.

Superyachts are boats over 24 metres. Prices start at around EUR 10,000 per day and go up to EUR 50,000 or more for a 50-metre with a full crew. These are usually booked weeks or months in advance.

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

Prices for a luxury yacht rental in Marbella vary by boat size, age, and season. Low season is November to March, mid-season is April to June and September to October, high season is July and August. Christmas and New Year are also high season.

As a rough guide, a 10-metre motor yacht costs from around EUR 800 per day in low season, EUR 1,200 in high season. A 15-metre motor yacht is EUR 1,500 to EUR 2,500. A 20-metre is EUR 3,000 to EUR 5,000. A 30-metre superyacht is EUR 10,000 to EUR 20,000. These prices include the skipper and fuel for 8 hours. Extras like food, drinks, water toys, and pier fees are extra.

Pier fees are usually EUR 100 to EUR 300, depending on the port and boat length. You pay this on top of the charter fee. Some operators include it, most don't. Check the listing.

You can also book half-day charters (5 hours) for about 70% of the full-day price. Some boats offer sunset cruises for 3 hours, from around EUR 500.

Best Time for a Luxury Yacht Rental in Marbella

The best time for a luxury yacht rental in Marbella is May to October. That's when the weather is warm, the sea is calm, and the beach clubs are open. July and August are the hottest, with temperatures reaching 35 degrees C. The sea temperature is around 24 degrees C. June and September are slightly cooler but still pleasant, with fewer crowds.

April and November can be hit or miss. You might get a warm day, or you might get wind and rain. The water is cooler, around 16 degrees C. December to March is winter, and most luxury charters are limited to sheltered areas. The Strait of Gibraltar can be rough in winter, with winds of 25 knots common.

If you want to see dolphins, the best months are April to June and September to October. That's when the pods are most active in the Strait. The dolphins are wild, so there's no guarantee, but most operators have a 90% success rate in those months.

How to Book a Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella on BoatHire24

Booking a luxury yacht rental in Marbella on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, enter your dates and group size, and filter by boat type, price, and port. You'll see real-time availability and prices. No fake availability or bait-and-switch.

Once you find a boat you like, click through to the listing. You'll see photos, specifications, a map of the departure port, and a list of included items. Read the terms carefully, especially the cancellation policy and any restrictions on food and drink.

To book, you pay a deposit (usually 30% to 50%) online. The balance is due 7 to 14 days before the charter. You'll receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact details and the exact meeting point. On the day, you meet the skipper at the port, they do a safety briefing, and you're off.

If you have questions before booking, you can use the contact form on each listing. The operator should reply within 24 hours. If they don't, contact us and we'll follow up.

Who Is a Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella For

A luxury yacht rental in Marbella is for anyone who wants a private day on the water without the hassle of owning a boat. It's for couples celebrating an anniversary, families with teenagers, groups of friends on a stag or hen do, and corporate clients entertaining clients.

It's not for budget travellers. A day on a luxury yacht costs several hundred euros per person, depending on the boat and group size. If you want a cheaper option, look at licence-free day boats or RIBs, which cost from around EUR 400 for half a day.

It's also not for people who get seasick easily. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but if you go west towards the Strait, the swell can be 1 to 2 metres. Take seasickness tablets an hour before departure if you're prone to it.

If you're looking for a party boat with a DJ and a foam machine, that's a different product. Some luxury yachts allow parties, but they usually charge a damage deposit of EUR 1,000 or more. Read the listing carefully.

What to Expect on the Day of Your Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

On the day of your luxury yacht rental in Marbella, you meet the skipper at the departure port at the agreed time. They'll check your ID and confirm the itinerary. The itinerary is flexible, but most charters follow a similar pattern: head out to a quiet cove for a swim, anchor for lunch, then cruise along the coast before returning.

Popular stops include Cala del Faro, a secluded beach near Marbella that's only accessible by boat. There's a small chiringuito on the beach that serves grilled fish and cold beer. Another favourite is the area around Cabopino, where the water is clear and there's a sandbank that forms at low tide.

If you want to see dolphins, the skipper can take you towards the Strait of Gibraltar. That's about 30 nautical miles from Puerto Banús, so it takes about an hour each way. The dolphins are common, but not guaranteed. The skipper will try their best.

, the skipper returns you to the port. They'll help you off the boat and you're free to go. Tips for the skipper are not mandatory but appreciated. EUR 50 to EUR 100 is standard for a day charter.

Frequently Asked Questions About Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

If you have more questions, check our blog for detailed guides on yacht charter etiquette, packing lists, and the best anchorages on the Costa del Sol.

Why Choose a Superyacht for Your Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

If you're looking at a luxury yacht rental Marbella and thinking big, a superyacht is the top end. These are boats over 24 metres, with a full crew that includes a captain, a chef, a stewardess, and sometimes a deckhand. You don't lift a finger. The chef prepares meals on board, the stewardess serves drinks, and the captain handles navigation. A 30-metre superyacht from Puerto Banús might have a master suite, three guest cabins, a flybridge with a Jacuzzi, and a beach club at the stern. Prices start at around EUR 10,000 per day and go up to EUR 50,000 for a 50-metre. These boats are usually booked for multi-day charters, but some operators offer single-day rentals if the schedule allows. You need to book at least two weeks in advance, more if you want a specific boat in July or August.

Local Anchorage Spots You Can Visit on a Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

One of the best parts of a luxury yacht rental Marbella is the freedom to anchor where you want. The coast from Benalmádena to Sotogrande has dozens of coves and beaches. Cala del Faro is a small beach near Marbella that's only reachable by boat. It has a chiringuito called El Faro that serves grilled sardines and cold beer. Another spot is the area off Cabopino, where the water is shallow and clear. You can anchor and swim to the sandbank that forms at low tide. Further west, near Estepona, there's the area around the Punta de la Doncella, where the water is calm and there's a reef for snorkelling. If you go east towards Benalmádena, you can anchor near the Selwo Marina and watch the dolphins from a distance. The skipper knows the best spots based on the wind and tide.

How Weather Affects Your Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella Charter

The Costa del Sol has a microclimate that makes it one of the sunniest places in Europe, but the weather still matters for a luxury yacht rental Marbella. The best conditions are from May to October, when the wind is light and the sea is calm. In July and August, the thermal wind picks up in the afternoon, blowing from the west at 10 to 15 knots. That's fine for most boats, but it can make the sea choppy near the Strait of Gibraltar. In winter, the wind can hit 25 knots from the east, which is called the Levante. That makes the sea rough and most luxury charters stay close to port. If you book in winter, ask the operator about their weather policy. Most will cancel or reschedule if the wind is above 20 knots. You get a full refund if they cancel, but if you cancel, you lose the deposit.

What to Wear and Bring for Your Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella

For a luxury yacht rental Marbella, you want to be comfortable but not sloppy. The crew expects you to dress like you're at a nice beach club, not a nightclub. Swimwear, a cover-up, and non-slip shoes are fine. Flip-flops are okay on deck, but the crew will ask you to remove them on the sunbeds to avoid scratches. Bring a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50, not spray, as it leaves a residue), a hat, and sunglasses. If you're prone to seasickness, take a tablet an hour before departure. The boat has a fridge and a cooler, so you can bring your own drinks, but check the policy on glass bottles. Some operators ban glass for safety. A light jacket is useful for the evening return, as the wind can pick up. Don't bring large suitcases or hard cases; the crew has limited storage space.

How to Combine a Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella with Other Activities

A luxury yacht rental Marbella doesn't have to be just a boat trip. You can combine it with other activities. Many operators offer a package that includes a meal at a chiringuito on the beach. The skipper will anchor near a restaurant and you take a small tender to the shore. Popular options include Los Tony's in Cabopino or El Faro in Cala del Faro. Another idea is to include a stop at the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins, then head to Tarifa for a walk through the old town. If you're in a superyacht, you can dock at the Sotogrande marina and go to the polo fields or the golf course. Some operators offer a sunset cruise that ends at a beach club in Puerto Banús, where you can have dinner on the terrace. You can also add water toys like a jet ski or a seabob for an extra fee, usually EUR 200 to EUR 500 per toy per day.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Yacht Charter Puerto Banús, Day & Sunset Hire
  • Boat Rental Marbella — Skipper Included from Puerto Banús
  • Catamaran Charter Marbella — Half & Full Day Cruises

faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a luxury yacht rental in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You don't need any experience or licence.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on a luxury yacht rental Marbella?
faqs.a: Most operators allow it, but some charge a corkage fee for outside alcohol. Check the listing details before you book.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a luxury yacht rental in Marbella?
faqs.a: For superyachts and high-season dates, book at least 4 weeks ahead. For smaller boats, 1 to 2 weeks is usually enough.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it's safe to go. If they cancel, you get a full refund or a reschedule. If you cancel because of weather, the cancellation policy applies.

faqs.q: Can I charter a luxury yacht for a wedding in Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes. Many boats allow weddings for an extra fee. You need to arrange catering, decorations, and an officiant yourself. Contact the operator for details.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the price of a luxury yacht rental Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes, fuel for up to 60 nautical miles is included. If you go further, you pay the difference, usually EUR 200 to EUR 500.

faqs.q: Can I book a luxury yacht for a half day or sunset cruise?
faqs.a: Yes. Half-day charters are 5 hours and cost about 70% of the full-day price. Sunset cruises are 3 hours and start from around EUR 500.

title: Fishing Charter Marbella — Deep Sea & Coastal Trips
metaDescription: Book a fishing charter Marbella for deep-sea and coastal trips. Compare boats with skippers from EUR 450. Catch bluefin tuna, sea bass, and more.
h1: Fishing Charter Marbella, Deep Sea & Coastal Trips
intro: If you want to catch real fish off the Costa del Sol, a fishing charter Marbella is the only way to do it. Forget the pier with a handline. You need a proper boat, a skipper who knows the grounds, and gear that won't snap when a 50 kg tuna hits. BoatHire24 lists boats from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, and Estepona, all with licensed skippers who speak English and know where the fish are hiding. Prices start from around EUR 450 for a half-day trip, and you don't need a fishing license yourself because the charter covers it.
bodyHtml:
What You Get on a Fishing Charter Marbella

A fishing charter Marbella includes the boat, the skipper, all fishing gear, bait, and usually ice to keep your catch fresh. The skipper handles the navigation, finds the fish using sonar and local knowledge, and helps you land them. You bring sun cream, a hat, and maybe a cooler bag if you plan to take fish home. Some charters offer food and drinks, but check the listing. Every boat on BoatHire24 is licensed for commercial passenger use, so insurance is covered. You don't need a fishing license for yourself because the charter's permit covers the whole trip.

Departure Ports for Your Trip

You can start a fishing charter Marbella from several ports. Puerto Banús is the most popular because it's close to deep water. From there you can reach the Marbella Ridge in 20 minutes, where the bottom drops to 200 metres. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a good option if you're staying in the town centre. Cabopino, near the dunes, gives quick access to the Cala del Faro area. Estepona is further west and gets you closer to the Strait of Gibraltar, where the current brings big pelagics. Sotogrande and Benalmádena are also available. Each port has its own advantages, depending on the season and what you want to catch.

Types of Boats Available

Motor yachts from 8 to 15 metres are the standard for a fishing charter Marbella. They have enough deck space for four to eight anglers, a cabin for shade, and a head. Catamarans are more stable, so if you get seasick, book one of those. RIBs and speedboats are faster and can get you to the fishing grounds in 15 minutes, but they have less space. For serious deep-sea fishing, you want a purpose-built fishing boat with outriggers, fighting chairs, and live bait wells. These are usually 10 to 14 metres and cost from EUR 600 for a half day. Sailing yachts are not ideal for fishing because the rigging gets in the way, but you can find them if you want a slow troll.

What Fish You Can Catch

The Costa del Sol has a mix of coastal and deep-sea species. In summer, bluefin tuna pass through the Strait of Gibraltar, and you can catch them from June to September. They run from 20 kg to over 100 kg. You'll also find dorado, amberjack, and bonito. In winter, the focus shifts to bottom fishing for sea bass, red snapper, and grouper. The Marbella Ridge and the area off Cabopino are good for sea bass. The drop-offs near Estepona hold big dentex and bream. If you go further out, past the 50 NM mark, you can hook swordfish at night. The skipper will know the best spots based on the season and the weather. Water temperature in summer hits 24 degrees C, which is when the tuna arrive.

Pricing Guidance for a Fishing Charter Marbella

Prices for a fishing charter Marbella vary by boat size, trip duration, and season. A half-day trip (four to five hours) on a standard motor yacht starts from around EUR 450 for up to six people. A full day (eight to ten hours) costs from EUR 800. For a purpose-built fishing boat with a fighting chair and live bait, expect EUR 600 to EUR 1,200 for a half day. Catamarans are slightly more expensive because of the stability and space. RIBs are cheaper, from EUR 350 for a half day, but you get less comfort. All prices include the skipper, fuel, gear, and bait. You don't need to tip, but 10 to 15 percent is normal if the skipper works hard. Check the listing on BoatHire24 for exact prices, and remember that peak season (July to August) can add 20 percent.

Best Seasons for Fishing

The season affects what you catch and how much you pay. Summer (June to September) is the peak for a fishing charter Marbella because of the tuna run. The weather is calm, with sea temperatures around 24 degrees C, and the fish are active. You'll also get dorado and amberjack. Winter (November to February) is quieter but still productive for bottom fishing. The sea can be rougher, with swells up to 2 metres, but the skipper will choose sheltered areas. Spring and autumn are the best compromises, with fewer crowds and good catches of sea bass and bream. If you want to avoid the heat and the crowds, book in May or October. The charter will run in any weather that's safe, but the skipper may cancel if a storm comes in.

How to Book a Fishing Charter Marbella

Booking a fishing charter Marbella on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, filter by port and boat type, and look for fishing-specific listings. Each listing shows the boat size, capacity, price, and what's included. You can message the owner directly to ask about specific fish species or trip duration. Payment is handled through the platform, so you're covered if something goes wrong. Book at least a week in advance in summer because charters sell out. For last-minute trips, check the availability calendar. You can also read the boat's description to see if it has outriggers or a live bait well. If you're not sure what to choose, contact the BoatHire24 team for a recommendation.

Who Should Book a Fishing Charter Marbella

A fishing charter Marbella is for anyone who wants to catch fish in the Mediterranean without owning a boat or buying a license. It works for beginners, because the skipper teaches you how to cast and reel. It works for experienced anglers who want a guided trip to the best spots. Families with kids can book a half-day trip on a catamaran, which is stable and has a toilet. Groups of friends can split the cost of a full-day trip and keep the catch. Corporate events are possible on larger boats that hold up to 12 people. If you're a solo angler, you can join a shared charter, but these are rare on the Costa del Sol. Most charters are private, so you pay for the whole boat.

What to Bring on the Day

For a fishing charter Marbella, bring sun cream (SPF 50), a hat, sunglasses, and a light jacket even in summer because the wind on the water is cooler. Wear non-slip shoes, not flip-flops. Bring water and snacks, though some charters provide them. If you want to take fish home, bring a cooler bag with ice. The charter provides all fishing gear, bait, and ice for the catch. Don't bring your own rod unless you have a specific preference, because the charter's gear is matched to the local fish. Seasickness tablets are a good idea if you're prone to it; take them an hour before departure. The skipper will give you a safety briefing before you leave the port.

Local Highlights Around Marbella

While you're in the area, Marbella offers plenty beyond the fishing. Puerto Banús is the main departure point, with its luxury yachts and designer shops. The La Concha mountain provides a backdrop that you can see from the water. The Río Verde estuary near Cabopino is a good spot for birdwatching before or after your trip. Estepona old town has whitewashed streets and a fishing port where you can buy fresh fish. If you have time, visit the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins and whales, or drive to the Nerja caves for a day trip. The fishing charter itself will give you a view of the coastline that most tourists never see.

FAQs About Fishing Charter Marbella

Do I need a fishing license for a fishing charter Marbella?

No. The charter's commercial license covers all passengers on board. You just show up and fish.

What's the minimum age for a fishing charter Marbella?

Most charters accept children from age 5. Check the boat listing for restrictions. Kids under 12 often go free or at a reduced rate.

Can I keep the fish I catch?

Yes, you can keep your catch within legal size and bag limits. The skipper will advise you. Some charters offer to clean and fillet the fish for you.

What happens if the weather is bad?

The skipper will cancel if conditions are unsafe, and you get a full refund or reschedule. Check the cancellation policy on BoatHire24.

How far offshore do you go on a fishing charter Marbella?

For coastal trips, you stay within 5 NM. For deep-sea, you go up to 20 NM. The skipper decides based on conditions and target species.

Can I book a private fishing charter Marbella?

Yes, all charters on BoatHire24 are private. You book the whole boat for your group. Prices are per boat, not per person.

What's the best time of day for fishing?

Early morning, from 7 am to 11 am, is best for most species. Afternoon trips work for bottom fishing. The skipper will suggest a departure time.

For more tips on fishing in the region, check out the BoatHire24 blog for seasonal guides and species profiles.

Fishing Techniques Used on Marbella Charters

On a fishing charter Marbella, the skipper decides the technique based on the target species and conditions. Trolling is common for tuna and dorado, with lures or natural bait towed behind the boat at 4 to 7 knots. Bottom fishing, or "jigging," targets sea bass and grouper over rocky reefs, using weighted lures dropped to the seabed at 30 to 80 metres. Live bait fishing, with sardines or mackerel caught on site, works for amberjack and dentex. Some charters offer spinning from the bow for bonito. The skipper will set up the gear and show you how to work the rod. You don't need experience, just a willingness to follow instructions. Each technique has its own feel, and the skipper will switch if the fish aren't biting.

Puerto Banús: The Best Starting Point for Deep-Sea Fishing

Puerto Banús is the most convenient port for a fishing charter Marbella because it sits right on the edge of the deep water. The harbour entrance leads directly to the Marbella Ridge, a submarine escarpment where the seabed drops from 50 metres to 200 metres within a mile. This structure attracts pelagic fish like tuna, amberjack, and dorado. From Puerto Banús, you can also head south-west toward the Cabo Pino lighthouse, where the rocky bottom holds bream and sea bass. The port has free parking for charter guests, and there are cafes and shops nearby for pre-trip supplies. If you're staying in Marbella town, the port is a 10-minute drive. The charter boats dock at the outer marina, so you can see them before you board.

Estepona: Gateway to the Strait of Gibraltar Fishing Grounds

Estepona is a strong alternative for a fishing charter Marbella if you want to target the big game fish that migrate through the Strait of Gibraltar. The port is 20 minutes west of Marbella, and from there you can reach the strait in about 40 minutes at 20 knots. The strait is a bottleneck where the Atlantic meets the Mediterranean, creating strong currents that push up nutrients and attract bluefin tuna, swordfish, and even marlin. The best months are July to September when the tuna run peaks. Estepona old town is worth a visit after your trip, with its flower-lined streets and fresh fish market. The charter boats here are usually purpose-built fishing vessels with outriggers and fighting chairs, designed for serious anglers.

Catamarans for Stable Fishing Trips

If you're prone to seasickness, a catamaran is the best choice for a fishing charter Marbella. The twin hulls reduce roll by up to 80 percent compared to a monohull, so you stay steady even in a 1-metre swell. Catamarans have a wide deck, giving you room to move around while casting or reeling. They're also quieter, which can help when fishing for wary species like sea bass. On a catamaran, you can fish from the bow, the stern, or the side platforms. The downsides are higher cost, from EUR 800 for a half day, and slower speed, around 15 to 18 knots. But if you want a comfortable day on the water with family or beginners, a catamaran is worth the extra money.

What to Expect on a Half-Day vs Full-Day Charter

A half-day fishing charter Marbella lasts four to five hours, usually departing at 8 am or 2 pm. You'll have time to reach the fishing grounds, fish for two to three hours, and return. This is enough for coastal species like sea bass or bonito. A full-day charter runs eight to ten hours, with a longer run to deep-water spots up to 20 NM offshore. You'll fish for five to seven hours, with a break for lunch. Full-day trips are better for tuna, swordfish, or a mixed bag of species. The cost difference is roughly double, but the catch potential is much higher. For beginners, a half day is enough to get the feel. For experienced anglers, a full day is the way to go.

How to Choose the Right Boat for Your Group

When booking a fishing charter Marbella, match the boat to your group size and experience level. For two to four people, a RIB or small motor yacht from 8 metres works well. For six to eight people, go for a 10 to 12 metre motor yacht or catamaran. Larger groups of up to 12 need a 14 to 15 metre boat. Check the deck layout: if you're trolling, you need space at the stern. If you're bottom fishing, a wide gunwale is helpful for holding rods. Ask the owner if the boat has outriggers, a live bait well, and a fish finder with GPS. Most boats on BoatHire24 list these details. If you're unsure, contact the owner through the platform for a recommendation.

Catch-and-Release and Conservation Practices

Many fishing charter Marbella operators encourage catch-and-release for certain species, especially large tuna and billfish. The skipper will advise you on handling the fish to minimise stress, using wet hands or a rubber mat, and releasing it quickly with a dehooking tool. For bottom fish like grouper, releasing them is simple because they swim back down. For tuna, the skipper may tag the fish for research before release. If you want to keep your catch, you can, but respect the legal limits: for example, bluefin tuna must be at least 30 kg to keep. The charter operator will have a scale and a measuring board. Practising catch-and-release helps maintain fish stocks for future trips, and it's a common practice on the Costa del Sol.

Cost Breakdown for a Fishing Charter Marbella

To give you a clearer picture, here's a typical cost breakdown for a fishing charter Marbella. A half-day trip on a 9-metre motor yacht for up to six people costs from EUR 450 to EUR 600. A full day on the same boat costs from EUR 800 to EUR 1,200. A purpose-built fishing boat with a fighting chair and live bait well costs from EUR 600 for a half day and EUR 1,000 for a full day. Catamarans start at EUR 800 for a half day and EUR 1,400 for a full day. RIBs are cheaper at EUR 350 to EUR 500 for a half day. All prices include the skipper, fuel, gear, bait, and ice. Peak season (July-August) adds 15 to 20 percent. You can negotiate a discount for a full-day booking or a group of eight or more.

Hidden Costs and Extras to Watch For

Most fishing charter Marbella listings are all-inclusive, but check for extras. Some charters charge extra for food and drinks, typically EUR 20 to EUR 30 per person. If you want the catch cleaned and filleted, some skippers do it for free, others charge EUR 10 to EUR 20. Fuel surcharges are rare, but if you're going far offshore, the owner may add a fee. Gratuity is not included, so budget 10 to 15 percent of the charter price for the skipper. If you book through BoatHire24, the listing will state what's included. Always confirm before booking to avoid surprises. The platform's messaging system lets you ask the owner directly about any extras.

How to Prepare for Seasickness on a Fishing Charter

Seasickness can ruin a fishing charter Marbella, especially if you're not used to the motion. The Mediterranean can have a gentle swell, but it's enough to upset some people. Take seasickness medication an hour before departure, such as cinnarizine or meclizine. Avoid heavy food and alcohol the night before. On the boat, stay on deck and focus on the horizon, not the water. If you feel queasy, tell the skipper, who can move to calmer water. Ginger biscuits or chewing gum can help. If you're prone to sickness, book a catamaran, which is more stable. Most charters have a first-aid kit with seasickness bags, but it's better to prevent it than deal with it.

What Happens After the Trip: Cleaning and Storing Your Catch

After a fishing charter Marbella, the skipper will help you handle your catch. They can clean and fillet the fish on the dock, usually for a small fee. You then take the fillets home in your cooler bag. If you're staying in a hotel or apartment, ask if they have a freezer you can use. Some charters offer to store your catch in their freezer until you leave. If you're flying home, check the airline's policy on transporting fish; most allow frozen fillets in checked luggage. For a group, you can split the catch among everyone. The skipper will advise on the best way to cook each species, from grilling tuna steaks to frying sea bass fillets.

faqs.q: Do I need a fishing license for a fishing charter Marbella?
faqs.a: No. The charter's commercial license covers all passengers on board. You just show up and fish.

faqs.q: What's the minimum age for a fishing charter Marbella?
faqs.a: Most charters accept children from age 5. Check the boat listing for restrictions. Kids under 12 often go free or at a reduced rate.

faqs.q: Can I keep the fish I catch?
faqs.a: Yes, you can keep your catch within legal size and bag limits. The skipper will advise you. Some charters offer to clean and fillet the fish for you.

faqs.a: The skipper will cancel if conditions are unsafe, and you get a full refund or reschedule. Check the cancellation policy on BoatHire24.

faqs.a: For coastal trips, you stay within 5 NM. For deep-sea, you go up to 20 NM. The skipper decides based on conditions and target species.
