faqs.q: Can I book a speedboat for a sunset cruise?
faqs.a: Yes, many operators offer sunset trips. They are usually 2-3 hours, departing around 18:00 in summer. The price is similar to a daytime rental. You can request a sunset cruise when booking through BoatHire24.

title: Yacht Rental Puerto Banus: Luxury Charters & Prices
metaDescription: Book a yacht rental Puerto Banus with a licensed skipper. Compare luxury motor yachts, catamarans & sailing boats. Prices from around EUR 1,500. Reserve online.
h1: Yacht Rental Puerto Banus: Luxury Charters & Prices
intro:
If you want to see the Costa del Sol from the water, a yacht rental Puerto Banus is the most direct way to do it. No queues, no crowded beaches, just you and the Mediterranean. Every boat we list comes with a licensed skipper, so you don't need any experience or a licence. You just turn up, step aboard, and decide where to go.

Puerto Banus is the superyacht capital of the coast, but you don't need a million-euro budget. We have boats from 8-metre RIBs to 50-metre motor yachts. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a mid-range motor yacht, and go up to price on request for the 30-metre-plus vessels. The key is knowing what you want and when you want it.
bodyHtml:
What to Expect from a Yacht Rental in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus is a purpose-built marina in Marbella, known for its designer shops, restaurants, and the sheer size of the yachts moored there. When you rent a yacht from this port, you are launching from the heart of the action. The marina has direct access to the open sea, so you are out on the water within ten minutes of leaving the dock.

Each charter includes a skipper who knows the local waters. They will handle navigation, mooring, and safety. You do not need to worry about fuel costs or cleaning up afterwards. The skipper will also recommend anchorages based on the weather and your preferences. Common stops include Cala del Faro, a quiet cove east of Marbella, and the waters off the Marbella Club Hotel for a bit of people-watching.

The boats available range from 8-metre RIBs for a fast, wet ride to 20-metre catamarans with plenty of deck space. Sailing yachts are also an option if you prefer a quieter, wind-powered experience. For larger groups, motor yachts from 15 to 30 metres are the standard choice, with multiple cabins, a flybridge, and a crew of two or three.

Which Ports Can You Depart From

While the page title focuses on Puerto Banus, BoatHire24 covers the whole Costa del Sol. You can book a yacht rental Puerto Banus and depart from the marina itself, or choose a different departure port if you are staying elsewhere. The options are:

  • Puerto Banus, The main hub for luxury charters. Direct access to the Mediterranean, close to Marbella town and the Golden Mile.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), A smaller, more relaxed marina in the centre of Marbella. Good for shorter trips and closer to the old town.
  • Cabopino, A quieter marina east of Marbella, near the Artola sand dunes. Ideal for a more private departure.
  • Estepona, A charming port west of Marbella, close to the old town and the Strait of Gibraltar. Good for dolphin-watching trips.
  • Sotogrande, A luxury residential marina further west, near the polo fields. Suitable for longer cruises towards Gibraltar.
  • Benalmádena, A tourist-friendly marina east of Marbella, with plenty of amenities and a lively atmosphere.

If you are staying in Puerto Banus, you will most likely depart from the main marina. The address is Puerto Banus, 29660 Marbella, Malaga. The marina has a large car park, but it fills up quickly in summer. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before your charter time.

Boat Types Available for Your Charter

Not all boats are the same. Here is a breakdown of what you can rent in Puerto Banus:

  • Motor yachts, The most popular choice. Typically 12 to 30 metres long, with a flybridge, sun pads, and a cabin. Prices from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 15-metre boat.
  • Catamarans, Wider and more stable, with a large deck area and a trampoline net at the bow. Good for groups who want to sunbathe and socialise. Prices from around EUR 2,000 for a half-day.
  • Sailing yachts, For a more traditional experience. Slower but quieter, and you can help the skipper if you want. Prices from around EUR 800 for a half-day on a 12-metre boat.
  • RIBs and speedboats, Fast, open boats for a thrilling ride. Usually 6 to 10 metres, with a powerful outboard engine. Prices from around EUR 400 for a half-day.
  • Fishing boats, Equipped with rods, bait, and a fish finder. The skipper will take you to the best spots. Prices from around EUR 600 for a half-day.
  • Licence-free day boats, Small motorboats (up to 8 metres) that you can drive yourself without a licence. They come with a basic safety briefing. Prices from around EUR 250 for a half-day.
  • Superyachts, Boats over 30 metres, with a professional crew, a chef, and luxury amenities. Price on request, typically starting at EUR 10,000 per day.

For a full list of available boats, visit our search page and filter by port and boat type.

Pricing Guidance for Yacht Rental Puerto Banus

Exact prices vary by boat, season, and duration. Here is a general guide based on typical rates in Puerto Banus:

  • Half-day (4 hours), From around EUR 1,500 for a 15-metre motor yacht. Catamarans from EUR 2,000. RIBs from EUR 400.
  • Full day (8 hours), From around EUR 2,500 for a 15-metre motor yacht. Catamarans from EUR 3,500. Superyachts from EUR 10,000.
  • Sunset charter (2-3 hours), From around EUR 800 for a motor yacht. RIBs from EUR 250.
  • Overnight or multi-day, Price on request. Most boats can accommodate 4-8 guests in cabins.

Prices include the skipper, fuel, and standard equipment. Food and drinks are usually extra, but some boats offer catering packages. You can also bring your own. The skipper will expect a tip of 10-15% of the charter fee, but this is not mandatory.

For exact pricing on a specific boat, use the enquiry form on the boat's page. We will get back to you within 24 hours with a firm quote.

Best Time of Year for a Yacht Charter in Puerto Banus

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate, with mild winters and hot summers. The best time for a yacht rental Puerto Banus is from May to October. During these months, the sea temperature is above 20 degrees C, and the air temperature ranges from 25 to 35 degrees C. July and August are the busiest, with higher prices and more boats on the water. If you want a quieter experience, book in May, June, or September.

Winter charters are possible, but the sea can be choppy and the air temperature drops to 15-20 degrees C. Many boats are still available, but you will need a jacket. The advantage is lower prices and fewer tourists.

Wind conditions vary. The prevailing wind is the Poniente, a westerly wind that can pick up in the afternoon. The skipper will adjust the route based on the forecast. For sailing yachts, a light breeze is ideal. For motor yachts, wind is less of an issue.

How to Book a Yacht Rental Puerto Banus

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You browse the available boats, select your preferred port (Puerto Banus or another), choose a date and duration, and send an enquiry. We will confirm availability and send you a payment link. A 50% deposit is required to secure the booking, with the balance due 7 days before the charter.

Cancellation policy: full refund if cancelled 14 days before the charter. 50% refund if cancelled 7 days before. No refund for cancellations within 7 days. If the skipper cancels due to weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule.

You can also contact us directly via the BoatHire24 homepage for a personalised quote. We work with all the major charter companies in Puerto Banus, so we can find a boat that fits your budget and group size.

Who Is This For

A yacht rental Puerto Banus is suitable for couples, families, and groups of friends. The boats can accommodate from 2 to 20 guests, depending on the size. If you are celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or corporate event, many boats offer catering and a sound system. The skipper can also arrange water toys like jet skis, paddleboards, and snorkelling gear for an extra fee.

If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared charter on some boats, but this is less common. Most charters are private, meaning you and your group are the only guests on board.

For more tips on planning your trip, read our blog about the best anchorages and restaurants on the Costa del Sol.

Local Highlights Near Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is a destination. The marina has a wide selection of restaurants, from tapas bars to Michelin-starred dining. The nearby La Concha mountain provides a dramatic backdrop, and the Rio Verde river flows into the sea just east of the port. If you have time before or after your charter, visit the old town of Marbella, about 10 minutes by taxi, with its narrow streets and orange trees.

For a longer trip, consider a charter that goes west towards the Strait of Gibraltar. Here you can spot dolphins and, in season, whales. The water is deeper and the coastline is more rugged. Or head east towards the Nerja caves, about an hour by boat. The caves are a popular tourist attraction, but you can only access them from the land side. The boat trip itself is scenic, passing by cliffs and coves.

What a Typical Half-Day Itinerary Looks Like

If you book a half-day yacht rental Puerto Banus, the skipper will usually propose a route that heads east towards Marbella or west towards Estepona. An eastbound trip might take you past the Marbella Club Hotel and the Golden Mile, stopping at Cala del Faro for a swim. The water there is clear and calm, and the cove is sheltered from the wind. You will anchor for 45 minutes to an hour, then cruise back to port. A westbound route goes past the San Pedro de Alcantara coast, with views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains. Some skippers will stop near the Guadalmina river mouth, where the water is shallow and warm. The total distance covered is around 10 to 15 nautical miles, depending on the speed of the boat. You will be back at the dock within 4 hours, with time to shower and change before dinner.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind

You need a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, the sun is strong), a hat, and sunglasses. Most boats have a cooler with ice, but you should bring your own drinks and snacks unless you order a catering package. Leave high heels at home, they damage the deck and the skipper will ask you to take them off. Soft-soled shoes or bare feet are fine. Do not bring glass bottles, many marinas and skippers ban glass for safety reasons. Plastic or metal containers only. If you plan to swim, bring a change of clothes and a dry bag for your phone. The boat will have a small fridge for perishables, but space is limited. For a full-day charter, pack a light lunch or arrange for the skipper to stop at a beach club. The crew can also recommend a local restaurant that delivers to the boat.

How the Skipper Affects Your Experience

The skipper is not just a driver. They know the local currents, wind patterns, and hidden spots that are not on the charts. A good skipper will adjust the speed and route based on your preferences. If you want to go fast and feel the spray, they will open the throttles. If you want a relaxed cruise with music and champagne, they will set a gentle pace. The skipper also handles all safety briefings and will point out landmarks like the La Concha mountain or the Marbella lighthouse. They will not join you for meals unless invited, but they will be on hand to answer questions. Most skippers speak English and Spanish, some speak German or French. If you have a specific language requirement, mention it when booking. The skipper works for the charter company, not for you directly, so tips are appreciated but not expected. A 10% tip is standard for good service.

What Happens in Bad Weather

The Costa del Sol has around 300 sunny days a year, but storms can happen, especially in autumn and winter. If the forecast shows high winds or heavy rain, the skipper will contact you 24 hours before the charter to discuss options. You can reschedule to a different day, change the route to a sheltered area, or cancel for a full refund. The skipper has the final say on safety. If the sea state is above 3 on the Beaufort scale, they may recommend staying in port or moving to a calmer anchorage like the Rio Verde estuary. Do not argue with the skipper, they are trained to assess risk. If you cancel due to weather, BoatHire24 will process your refund within 5 working days. If you decide to go ahead in marginal conditions, the experience will be bumpy and you may feel seasick. Take motion sickness tablets an hour before departure if you are prone to nausea.

How to Choose Between a Motor Yacht and a Catamaran

Motor yachts are faster and more luxurious, with a flybridge that gives you a panoramic view. They are better for couples or small groups who want a stylish, high-speed experience. Catamarans are wider and more stable, with a large deck area that does not tilt when anchored. They are better for families with children or groups of friends who want to move around without feeling the motion of the boat. Catamarans also have a trampoline net at the bow, which is a popular spot for sunbathing. The trade-off is speed: a catamaran cruises at around 8 to 12 knots, while a motor yacht can do 20 to 30 knots. If your priority is covering distance or arriving quickly at a specific cove, go for a motor yacht. If you want to spend most of the day at anchor, swimming and relaxing, a catamaran is the better choice. Both types include a skipper, so you do not need to worry about handling the boat.

What About Fuel and Extra Costs

Fuel is included in the charter price for most boats on BoatHire24. This is standard for the Costa del Sol market. However, if you request a longer route or multiple stops that require extra engine hours, the skipper may charge a fuel supplement. This is rare for half-day or full-day charters within the usual range. Other extra costs include water toys like jet skis (from around EUR 200 per hour), paddleboards (EUR 50 per hour), and snorkelling gear (EUR 20 per set). Some boats have a sound system that you can connect via Bluetooth. If you want a DJ or live music, that is an additional cost, around EUR 300 for a half-day. Catering packages range from EUR 30 to EUR 80 per person, depending on the menu. You can also order from a local restaurant and have it delivered to the dock before departure. The crew will plate and serve it for you. Gratuities are not included, so budget EUR 100 to EUR 300 for the crew, depending on the charter value.

How Far Can You Go in a Half-Day or Full-Day Charter

From Puerto Banus, a half-day charter (4 hours) typically covers 10 to 15 nautical miles. Eastbound, you can reach the Marbella coast and Cala del Faro. Westbound, you can reach Estepona port. A full-day charter (8 hours) extends your range to 25 to 40 nautical miles. Eastbound, you can reach the Nerja area, but you will not have time to stop at the caves. Westbound, you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar and the dolphin-watching zones near Tarifa. The skipper will plan the route to maximise your time on the water and minimise fuel consumption. If you want to go further, consider a multi-day charter with overnight stops. The boat will have cabins and a galley. Popular overnight destinations include Gibraltar (20 nautical miles from Puerto Banus) and Ceuta (25 nautical miles across the strait). Both require a passport for disembarkation.

What Makes Puerto Banus Different from Other Marinas

Puerto Banus is the most famous marina on the Costa del Sol. It was built in the 1970s and is synonymous with luxury and glamour. The marina has over 900 berths, many of which are occupied by superyachts. The atmosphere is lively, with designer shops, high-end restaurants, and nightclubs. The departure procedure is efficient: you check in at the captain's office, then walk to your boat. The marina has a fuel dock and a water refill station. The main advantage of departing from Puerto Banus is the proximity to the open sea. Unlike Marbella Marina, which is closer to the town centre, Puerto Banus is right on the coast. You are out of the marina and into the Mediterranean within 5 minutes. The downside is the crowds. In July and August, the marina is packed with tourists and boat traffic. If you prefer a quieter departure, consider Cabopino or Estepona. Both are within 20 minutes by taxi from Puerto Banus.

faqs.a: No. Every boat on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any experience or a licence. The only exception is licence-free day boats, which are small motorboats you can drive yourself without a licence.

faqs.q: How much does a yacht rental in Puerto Banus cost?
faqs.a: Prices vary by boat size and season. For a half-day on a 15-metre motor yacht, expect from around EUR 1,500. Catamarans from EUR 2,000. RIBs from EUR 400. Superyachts are price on request, typically starting at EUR 10,000 per day.

faqs.q: What is included in the price?
faqs.a: The price includes the skipper, fuel, and standard safety equipment. Food and drinks are usually extra. Some boats offer catering packages. You can also bring your own. A tip of 10-15% for the skipper is customary but not required.

faqs.q: Can I choose the departure port?
faqs.a: Yes. While the page focuses on Puerto Banus, you can depart from any of our ports: Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Just specify your preferred port when booking.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper will cancel the charter if conditions are unsafe. In that case, you get a full refund or can reschedule. If you cancel due to weather, our standard cancellation policy applies.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a yacht?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. RIBs typically carry 6-8 guests. Motor yachts and catamarans can carry 10-20 guests. Superyachts can carry up to 30 guests. Check the boat's capacity on its page.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: In peak season (July and August), book at least 2 weeks in advance. In shoulder months, 1 week is usually enough. For superyachts, book 1-2 months ahead to secure availability.

title: Boat Hire Fuengirola: Affordable Rentals for Your Day Out
metaDescription: Book boat hire Fuengirola with BoatHire24. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs from Cabopino to Marbella. All charters include a skipper. Reserve online.
h1: Boat Hire Fuengirola: Rent a Boat with Skipper Today
intro: If you are looking for boat hire Fuengirola, you have come to the right place. Fuengirola sits on the Costa del Sol, about 30 minutes from Marbella, and its marina is a launching point for day trips along the coast. But here is the catch: Fuengirola itself does not have a large charter fleet. Most boats for hire near Fuengirola are based at Cabopino Marina (10 minutes east) or Puerto Banús (20 minutes west). BoatHire24 lets you search both ports and more, so you get the best boat for your day out without the hassle of calling around.
bodyHtml:
What Does Boat Hire Fuengirola Include?

Every charter you book through BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper. That means you do not need a boating licence or any experience. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety, so you can relax, swim, or sunbathe. Fuel, port fees, and cleaning are usually included in the quoted price, but check the listing details for your chosen boat. Standard inclusions are a cooler with ice, snorkelling gear, and a Bluetooth speaker. Some larger yachts also offer paddleboards, a jet ski, or a crew member for food service.

Nearest Ports for Fuengirola Boat Hire

Your closest option for boat hire near Fuengirola is Cabopino Marina. It is a small, quiet port with direct access to the sea and no waiting time to exit. Cabopino has a handful of motor yachts (8-12 metres) and sailing yachts. From there you can head to the sandy beach at Cabopino or east to Cala del Moral. Next is Puerto Banús, 20 minutes west by road. That is the largest charter hub on the Costa del Sol, with hundreds of boats from 6-metre RIBs to 40-metre superyachts. Marbella Marina is a bit further west and has a good mix of catamarans and motor yachts. Estepona and Sotogrande are 30-40 minutes further west but offer larger yachts and longer cruising ranges. Benalmádena, east of Fuengirola, also has a small selection of fishing boats and day boats.

Types of Boats Available for Hire

Motor yachts are the most popular choice for Fuengirola boat hire. You can find 8-metre day cruisers for small groups and 15-metre flybridge yachts for up to 12 guests. Prices start from around EUR 500 for a half day on a small motor yacht. Catamarans are stable and spacious, ideal for families or groups of 8-12. A typical 12-metre catamaran costs from EUR 800 for a half day. Sailing yachts are slower but quieter; a 10-metre sailing yacht with skipper costs from EUR 400 for half a day. RIBs and speedboats are the cheapest option, from EUR 300 for a 2-hour blast along the coast. Fishing boats are available for sport fishing trips, usually half-day or full-day, from EUR 600. Licence-free day boats (under 6 metres) are rare in this area because most charters include a skipper anyway. Superyachts over 20 metres are available from Puerto Banús, price on request.

Pricing Guidance for Fuengirola Boat Hire

Prices vary by boat type, duration, and season. Here is a rough guide for 2025: half-day (4 hours) on a 8-metre motor yacht costs from EUR 500. Full day (8 hours) on the same boat is from EUR 800. A 12-metre catamaran half-day is from EUR 800, full day from EUR 1,300. A 6-metre RIB for 2 hours is from EUR 300. A sport fishing boat half-day is from EUR 600. All prices include skipper, fuel, VAT, and basic extras. Peak season (July-August) prices can be 20-30% higher. You can compare prices on our search page.

Best Time to Hire a Boat in Fuengirola

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days a year. The best months for boating are May to October, when sea temperatures are above 18 degrees C and winds are light. July and August are the busiest and hottest, with temperatures up to 35 degrees C. September and October are still warm but quieter. Winter (November to March) is possible but cooler and windier; some boats still operate but you will have fewer choices. Spring (April-May) is pleasant with fewer crowds. For dolphin watching, the Strait of Gibraltar is best from April to October, but you can see them year-round closer to Fuengirola.

What to Do on a Boat Trip from Fuengirola

Most boat hire Fuengirola trips include swimming stops, lunch at a beach club, and sightseeing. Popular destinations: Cabopino beach (east), with its sand dunes and clear water. Cala del Faro (west of Marbella) is a rocky cove with good snorkelling. Puerto Banús is worth a cruise-by to see the superyachts. For a longer trip, head to the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins and pilot whales. Some charters go as far as the Nerja caves (east), but that is a full-day trip from Fuengirola. Many boats also offer sunset cruises with cava and music.

How to Book Boat Hire Fuengirola

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to our BoatHire24 homepage, enter your preferred port (Cabopino, Puerto Banús, or Marbella) and your dates. Filter by boat type, guest number, and budget. Click on a listing to see photos, full description, and availability. You can book and pay online with a deposit (usually 30-50%) and the balance due on the day. You will receive a confirmation email with meeting point and skipper contact. Cancel up to 48 hours before for a full refund on most listings.

Who Is Boat Hire Fuengirola For?

Anyone can book a boat in Fuengirola. It is suitable for families with children (all boats have life jackets), couples, groups of friends, and corporate events. If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared charter or book a small RIB. If you are a non-swimmer, you can still enjoy the cruise and views. If you want to learn sailing, some skippers offer basic tuition. For bachelor/bachelorette parties, many boats allow DJ equipment and decorations (check with the skipper). For business meetings, larger yachts have indoor saloons with air conditioning.

Why Book with BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace, not a single company. We list boats from multiple operators so you can compare prices, sizes, and amenities. All skippers are licensed and insured. We have been operating since 2018 and have helped thousands of customers. Our blog has guides on the best beaches, restaurants, and tips for your day out. We do not add hidden fees; the price you see is the price you pay. And if you have questions, our support team responds within a few hours.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: Understanding the Local Coastline

The stretch of coast between Fuengirola and Cabopino is about 5 nautical miles of mostly sandy beaches backed by low-rise hotels and restaurants. The water here is calm most days because the shoreline faces south-east, sheltered from the prevailing south-westerly winds. If you launch from Cabopino Marina, you can be in open water within 5 minutes. Head east and you reach the Torreblanca area, where the seabed drops off quickly to 10-15 metres, good for snorkelling. West from Cabopino takes you past the Cala del Moral, a small cove with a beach bar that only boats can access. That cove is about 2 NM from Cabopino, a 10-minute cruise on a motor yacht.

Why Cabopino Marina Is the Best Starting Point for Fuengirola

Cabopino Marina is small, with about 250 berths, but it has direct sea access without any lock gates or waiting times. You drive right to the pontoon, park your car in the free public car park (50 metres away), and step onto the boat. Compare that to Puerto Banús, where parking costs EUR 30-50 and you often queue to exit the marina. Cabopino has a decent chiringuito (beach restaurant) called Chiringuito Cabopino, which serves grilled fish and cold beer. The marina also has a fuel dock, a small supermarket, and public toilets. Boats available from Cabopino include motor yachts from 7 to 12 metres, sailing yachts up to 10 metres, and a couple of RIBs. For larger yachts or catamarans, you will need to go to Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: A Typical Half-Day Itinerary

Say you book a 9-metre motor yacht from Cabopino for a half-day (10:00 to 14:00). You meet the skipper at the pontoon, he gives a 5-minute safety briefing, and you cast off. He motors east at 8-10 knots, passing the Fuengirola beachfront. After 15 minutes, you reach the Cala del Moral. The skipper drops anchor in 5 metres of clear water. You jump in with snorkel gear, swim to the beach for a drink at the chiringuito, and return to the boat. Then you cruise back west past Cabopino towards the Marbella coastline, passing the luxury villas of Los Monteros. You stop again for a swim near the Marbella Club hotel. By 13:30, you head back to Cabopino, arriving at 14:00. That is a relaxed 4-hour trip covering about 10 NM total. Fuel cost for that trip is around EUR 50-80, included in the charter price.

What About the Weather in Fuengirola for Boating

The Costa del Sol has a microclimate that keeps the coast warmer than inland. Summer sea breezes are common, blowing from the south-west at 10-15 knots in the afternoon. That is fine for motor yachts and catamarans but can make sailing choppy for small RIBs. Mornings are usually dead calm, which is why most half-day charters start at 10:00. The Levante wind (easterly) can blow in autumn and winter, pushing waves up to 1.5 metres near the coast. If the Levante is forecast, skippers will advise you to reschedule. The Poniente (westerly) is gentler. Check the wind forecast on Windy or AEMET before your trip. BoatHire24 skippers monitor the weather and will cancel if conditions are unsafe, with a full refund or reschedule.

Fuengirola Boat Hire for Fishing: What You Need to Know

If you want to fish, you can hire a dedicated fishing boat from Fuengirola or Cabopino. These boats are typically 7-9 metre RIBs or small motor yachts with rod holders, a fish finder, and a live bait tank. The skipper knows the local fishing spots: the rocky reefs off Cala del Moral, the artificial reef near the Fuengirola river mouth, and the deeper waters 3 NM offshore. Common catches include sea bass, bream, mackerel, and sometimes dentex. A half-day fishing charter costs from EUR 600 for up to 4 anglers. You keep your catch (subject to size limits). The skipper cleans and bags the fish for you. You do not need a fishing licence because the boat has a commercial licence. Book a fishing charter through the search page and filter by "fishing".

Sunset Cruises from Fuengirola: A Low-Cost Option

Sunset cruises are popular in summer. You depart around 19:00, cruise for 2-3 hours, and watch the sun set behind La Concha mountain. The light turns the sea orange and pink. Most sunset cruises include a glass of cava or beer and some snacks. Prices start from around EUR 200 for a small RIB for 2 people, up to EUR 600 for a motor yacht for 10 people. Some operators offer a "sunset and tapas" option, where you stop at a beach club for dinner. From Fuengirola, the best sunset views are west towards Marbella and Puerto Banús. You can book a sunset cruise directly on BoatHire24 by selecting "sunset" as the activity type.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: Group Sizes and Legal Limits

Spanish law limits the number of passengers on a charter boat. For boats under 12 metres, the maximum is usually 10-12 guests plus the skipper. For boats 12-20 metres, it can be 12-14 guests. For superyachts over 20 metres, it can be up to 36 guests. The skipper counts these numbers strictly; you cannot exceed the limit. If you have a group of 15, you need a catamaran or a large motor yacht. If you have a group of 20, you need a superyacht from Puerto Banús. Check the "max guests" field on each listing before booking. For large groups, book at least 2 weeks in advance in summer.

How to Get to the Ports from Fuengirola

Cabopino Marina is 5 km east of Fuengirola town centre, a 10-minute drive. You can take a taxi for around EUR 12-15, or the M-220 bus from Fuengirola bus station to the Cabopino stop. Puerto Banús is 12 km west, a 20-minute drive, taxi cost EUR 20-25. Marbella Marina is 15 km west, taxi EUR 25-30. If you are staying in Fuengirola, Cabopino is the most convenient. If you are staying in Mijas Costa or La Cala de Mijas, Cabopino is also close. For larger groups, consider hiring a minibus or using Uber (available in Marbella but not always in Fuengirola). BoatHire24 can provide skipper contact details for meeting at the port.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: What to Bring and What Not to Bring

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50, water-resistant), a towel, sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the wind. Swimwear and a change of clothes are useful. Bring your own food and drinks unless the charter includes catering. Most boats have a small fridge and a sink. Do not bring glass bottles onto the boat; some skippers ban them for safety. Do not bring large coolers; the boat provides one. Do not bring illegal substances; the skipper can refuse to sail if he suspects drug use. Do not bring high heels; they damage the deck. Do bring a waterproof phone case or a dry bag. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness tablet 30 minutes before departure; the water is usually calm, but the Strait of Gibraltar can be choppy.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: Payment and Cancellation Policies

When you book on BoatHire24, you pay a deposit online (typically 30-50% of the total). The balance is paid in cash or by card to the skipper on the day. Some operators accept bank transfer for the balance. Cancellation policy: most listings offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, you lose the deposit. If the skipper cancels due to weather or mechanical issues, you get a full refund or can reschedule. Read the cancellation policy on each listing before booking. For peak season, consider booking with a flexible cancellation option if your plans are uncertain.

Fuengirola Boat Hire: A Note on Licence-Free Boats

Some visitors ask about licence-free boats (boats under 6 metres that you can drive without a licence). In Fuengirola, these are rare because most charter companies prefer to include a skipper for safety and insurance reasons. You might find a small 5-metre RIB with a 40 HP engine that you can rent without a skipper, but the rental company will give you a 15-minute briefing and require a credit card deposit. These are available from a few operators in Cabopino and Benalmádena. Prices start from around EUR 150 for 2 hours. However, we recommend booking a boat with a skipper for your first trip; it is safer and more relaxing. You can search for licence-free options on BoatHire24 by filtering "no licence required".

Fuengirola Boat Hire: Combining with Nearby Attractions

If you have a full-day charter, you can combine your boat trip with a visit to the Nerja caves. From Fuengirola, it is about 25 NM east, a 1.5-hour cruise at 15 knots. You moor at the Nerja harbour (a small fishing port) and take a taxi to the caves (10 minutes). The caves are open 10:00-18:00, entrance fee EUR 10. Alternatively, head west to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin watching. From Cabopino to the Strait is about 50 NM, a 3-hour cruise. Most skippers will do this as a full-day trip (8 hours) with a dolphin guarantee. Another option is to stop at the beach club in Cala del Faro for lunch. That cove has a restaurant called El Faro that serves paella and grilled sardines. You can anchor and take the dinghy to shore.

  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a boat in Fuengirola?
faqs.a: No. All our charters include a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax and enjoy the trip.

faqs.q: How much does boat hire Fuengirola cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 300 for a 2-hour RIB trip, EUR 500 for a half-day motor yacht, and EUR 800 for a half-day catamaran. Full-day rates are higher.

faqs.q: What is the closest port to Fuengirola for boat hire?
faqs.a: Cabopino Marina is the closest, about 10 minutes east of Fuengirola. Puerto Banús is 20 minutes west and has the largest selection.
