faqs.a: No. The price includes the skipper, fuel, insurance, and listed extras. You may need to pay a security deposit for damages, refunded after the trip.

title: Boat Rental Marbella Port: Easy Access & Options
metaDescription: Book a boat rental Marbella port departure from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs with a licensed skipper included. Check prices now.
h1: Boat Rental Marbella Port: Your Guide to Departure Options
intro:
If you are searching for a boat rental Marbella port departure, the key question is which marina you start from. Marbella has two main ports: the glitzy Puerto Banús and the more relaxed Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo). Both offer direct access to the Costa del Sol coastline, but the vibe, parking, and boat selection differ. This page walks you through what each port offers, what you get for your money, and how to pick the right boat for your day out. No fluff, just the details you need to decide.

Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper, so you do not need a boating licence. You just turn up, step aboard, and let the captain handle the navigation. Whether you want a fast RIB for a dolphin-spotting run to the Strait of Gibraltar, a catamaran for a sunset cruise, or a motor yacht for a family day at anchor near Cabopino, the port you choose affects the itinerary and the price. We cover the lot below.
bodyHtml:
Why the Port Matters for Your Boat Rental Marbella Port Trip

Marbella port is not one single location. Puerto Banús is the famous superyacht hub with designer shops, flashy cars, and a party atmosphere. Marbella Marina, a few kilometres east, is quieter, with a fishing harbour vibe, a long promenade, and more affordable mooring fees. Your departure port determines the first 15 minutes of your charter: from Puerto Banús you head west towards the Río Verde and the exclusive villas of the Golden Mile, while from Marbella Marina you can quickly reach the Cala del Faro or head east towards Cabopino and the sand dunes. Both ports give you access to the same sea, but the scenery and the crowd differ. If you want a rental that starts with a stroll past 50-metre superyachts, pick Puerto Banús. If you prefer a low-key start with easier parking, choose Marbella Marina.

What Is Included in a Boat Rental Marbella Port Charter

When you book a boat rental Marbella port through BoatHire24, the standard package includes the vessel, a professional skipper, fuel for a typical day trip, and basic safety equipment. Most charters also supply a cooler with ice, a Bluetooth speaker, and snorkelling gear. You do not pay extra for the skipper’s time, but you should budget for food and drinks you bring yourself or order as a catering add-on. Some larger motor yachts and catamarans offer a hostess or a chef for an additional fee. Check the boat listing for the exact inclusions. The skipper will stay with the boat for the whole charter, so you can relax and swim without worrying about anchoring or returning on time.

Puerto Banús: The Glamorous Start for Your Boat Rental Marbella Port

Puerto Banús is the most famous port on the Costa del Sol. Built in the 1970s around a fishing village, it now hosts some of the largest superyachts in the Mediterranean. Departing from here gives you immediate views of La Concha mountain and the luxury developments of the Golden Mile. The port has dozens of bars and restaurants right on the waterfront, so you can grab a coffee before boarding. Boat options in Puerto Banús range from 30-foot motor yachts costing around EUR 800 for a half-day to 60-foot catamarans at EUR 1,500 for a full day. RIBs and speedboats are also available from about EUR 500 for a half-day. The port is busy, so allow 15 minutes to find the berth. Parking is expensive (around EUR 30 for a few hours) and limited; consider a taxi or a transfer service.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): The Practical Choice

Marbella Marina, also called Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, sits just east of the old town. It is less flashy than Puerto Banús but more convenient for most visitors. The marina has free parking for the first hour and reasonable rates after that. The promenade has a few casual chiringuitos and a small supermarket where you can stock up on supplies. Boats here tend to be slightly cheaper than in Puerto Banús because the mooring fees are lower. You will find fishing boats, licence-free day boats (boats under 6 metres that do not require a licence in Spain), and family-friendly catamarans. A half-day fishing charter from Marbella Marina starts at around EUR 400. The departure is straightforward, and within 10 minutes you are in open water heading towards Cabopino or the Cala del Faro.

Boat Types Available for Your Marbella Port Rental

You can choose from five main categories when you book a boat rental Marbella port. Motor yachts are the most popular: sleek, fast, and comfortable, with a cabin, a bathroom, and a sun deck. Sizes range from 25 feet to 60 feet. Prices for a full-day motor yacht start from around EUR 900. Catamarans offer more deck space and stability, ideal for groups or families with children. A 40-foot catamaran for a full day costs from EUR 1,300. Sailing yachts are quieter and cheaper, from EUR 600 for a full day, but they are slower and have less shade. RIBs and speedboats are the fastest option, hitting 30-40 knots, perfect for a quick trip to see dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar. A half-day RIB charter costs from EUR 500. Licence-free day boats are the cheapest, starting at EUR 300 for a half-day, but they are limited to nearshore cruising and have no cabin. Finally, superyachts over 20 metres are available for luxury charters; prices are on request and start from around EUR 5,000 per day.

Pricing Guidance for Boat Rental Marbella Port

Prices depend on the boat size, type, season, and duration. A half-day (4 hours) is the minimum for most charters. Full-day (8 hours) gives you time to anchor, swim, and have lunch. High season (June to September) sees the highest rates. For a motor yacht from Marbella port, expect to pay EUR 800 to EUR 2,000 for a half-day and EUR 1,500 to EUR 4,000 for a full day. Catamarans are similar. RIBs cost EUR 500 to EUR 1,200 for a half-day. Licence-free boats are the cheapest at EUR 300 to EUR 600 for a half-day. Superyachts are price on request. Always check the listing for fuel policy: some charters include fuel for a set number of hours, others charge extra. The total cost usually includes VAT (21% in Spain) and port fees, but confirm with the operator. For a full list of available boats, use the search page to filter by port and date.

Best Seasons for a Marbella Port Boat Rental

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days a year. The boating season runs from April to October. In April and May, temperatures are 20-25 degrees C, the water is cool (16-18 degrees C), and the sea is calm. June to August is peak season: 30-35 degrees C, water 22-26 degrees C, and the coast is busy. September and October are warm (25-30 degrees C) with fewer crowds. Winter charters are possible on calm days, but many operators reduce availability. For the best balance of weather and price, consider late May or late September. If you are flexible, check the blog for seasonal tips and local events that affect availability.

How to Book a Boat Rental Marbella Port with BoatHire24

Booking is straightforward. Go to the search page, enter your date, group size, and preferred port (Marbella Marina or Puerto Banús). Browse the listings, check the photos, and read the boat specifications. Each listing shows the price, duration, skipper language, and included extras. Click to book, pay a deposit (usually 30% to 50%), and the operator confirms the time and berth. You pay the balance on the day, in cash or card, directly to the skipper. Cancellation policies vary: most operators offer a full refund if you cancel at least 7 days before. For last-minute bookings (within 48 hours), contact the operator directly through the platform. Remember that the skipper will contact you the day before to confirm the meeting point and any weather updates.

Who Is a Marbella Port Boat Rental For

A boat rental Marbella port suits a wide range of visitors. Couples enjoy a private sunset cruise with champagne. Families appreciate the safe swimming spots near Cabopino and the Cala del Faro. Groups of friends use the boat as a floating party venue with music and drinks. Business clients book a luxury motor yacht for corporate events or client entertainment. Solo travellers can join a shared charter (if available) to meet people and split the cost. The only people who should not book are those who get severe seasickness (the Mediterranean is usually calm, but the Strait can be choppy) and those who want to drive the boat themselves without a licence (the skipper is mandatory). If you want to learn to sail, some operators offer tuition charters, but that is a different product.

Local Highlights You Can Reach from Marbella Port

Departing from Marbella port opens up several coastal attractions. Heading west from Puerto Banús, you pass the Río Verde estuary and the exclusive beach clubs of the Golden Mile. Further west, you reach the Cala del Faro, a quiet cove with a lighthouse, good for snorkelling. Continuing to Estepona old town, you can anchor near the marina and walk into the white-washed streets for lunch. East from Marbella Marina, you reach Cabopino with its sand dunes and the nearby nudist beach. Further east, the coast becomes rocky until you reach the Nerja caves (about 40 nautical miles, a full-day trip). The most popular short trip is to the Strait of Gibraltar, about 20 NM from Puerto Banús, where you can spot pilot whales, common dolphins, and occasionally orcas. The Strait is open ocean and can be rough, so a RIB or a fast motor yacht is best for that trip.

Practical Tips for Your Boat Rental Marbella Port Day

Arrive at the port 15 minutes before the scheduled departure. Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a towel, and a change of clothes. Swimwear is essential. Most boats have a bathroom, but it is a marine toilet, so do not flush anything other than toilet paper. Food and drink: you can bring your own, or order a catering package (EUR 20-40 per person for a platter and drinks). The skipper will advise on the best anchor spots based on the wind and swell. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness tablet an hour before departure. Tipping the skipper is not mandatory but is appreciated; 10% of the charter fee is standard for good service. Finally, remember that the charter includes the skipper, so you cannot take the helm unless you hold a valid Spanish recreational boating licence.

Comparing Departure Times: Morning vs Afternoon Charters

The time you choose for your boat rental Marbella port trip affects the sea conditions and the experience. Morning charters (9 AM to 1 PM) give you the calmest sea, especially in summer when the afternoon Levante wind picks up. The water is glassy, visibility for snorkelling is best, and you can anchor at Cala del Faro before the crowds arrive. Afternoon charters (2 PM to 6 PM) are warmer and better for swimming, but expect a light chop after 3 PM. Sunset charters (6 PM to 9 PM) are popular for couples, with golden light on La Concha mountain and a cooler breeze. The trade-off is that you cannot swim after dark, and some anchorages close at sunset. If you want to combine swimming with sunset, book a full-day charter from 11 AM to 7 PM.

Fuel Costs and Hidden Fees in Marbella Port Rentals

Fuel is a variable cost that can catch you out. Most boat rental Marbella port listings include fuel for a standard day (4-6 hours of cruising and idling). But if you take a long trip to the Strait of Gibraltar or Nerja, the skipper may charge extra for fuel beyond the included amount. Ask before booking: the rate is typically EUR 1.50 to EUR 2.00 per litre for diesel or petrol. A RIB burning 80 litres per hour at cruising speed can add EUR 160 to a half-day trip. Some operators charge a cleaning fee of EUR 30-50 if you leave the boat messy. Port departure fees are usually included in the price, but check the fine print. For a transparent experience, use the BoatHire24 platform where operators list total prices upfront.

Licence-Free Day Boats: A Budget Option from Marbella Marina

If you want to drive the boat yourself without a licence, licence-free day boats are available from Marbella Marina. These are small motorboats under 6 metres, with a 15-30 HP engine, and a maximum capacity of 5-6 people. They cost from around EUR 300 for a half-day. The operator gives you a 10-minute briefing on the controls and local navigation rules. You are restricted to nearshore waters within 2 nautical miles of the coast, so you cannot cross to Africa or visit the Strait. The boats have no cabin, no toilet, and limited shade. They are fine for a relaxed afternoon exploring the coves between Marbella and Cabopino. Do not expect speed: these boats top out at 6-8 knots. Book in advance in summer, as there are only a few available.

Fishing Charters from Marbella Port: What You Actually Catch

Fishing charters from Marbella port target different species depending on the season. From April to June, you can catch amberjack, sea bass, and bream near the rocky bottoms off Cabopino. From July to September, the warmer water brings bonito and small tuna closer to shore. Bottom fishing for grouper and snapper is productive year-round near the artificial reefs. A half-day fishing charter costs from EUR 400 for up to 4 people, including rods, bait, and a skipper who cleans your catch. Most charters are catch-and-release unless you want to keep a few for dinner. The local restaurants in Puerto Banús will cook your catch for a fee of around EUR 15 per person. Do not expect trophy-sized fish; the Mediterranean is overfished, but a day on the water with a cold beer is still a good time.

Group Size Limits and How They Affect Your Marbella Port Rental

Boat capacity is strictly enforced by the Spanish coast guard. A 30-foot motor yacht typically holds 8-10 passengers plus the skipper. A 40-foot catamaran holds 12-14. RIBs carry 6-8. Exceeding the limit invalidates the insurance and can result in fines or the charter being cancelled. If your group has 15 or more people, you need two boats or a superyacht over 20 metres. For a large group, consider booking two motor yachts from Puerto Banús that can raft together at anchor. The cost per person drops if you split the total across the group. For example, a EUR 1,500 catamaran shared among 12 people costs EUR 125 each. Use the search page to filter by passenger capacity and compare prices per head.

Weather Cancellations and Refund Policies for Marbella Port Charters

The skipper has the final decision on weather. If the forecast shows winds over 20 knots or a thunderstorm, they may cancel for safety. In that case, you get a full refund or a reschedule. If you cancel because of personal reasons, the policy depends on the operator. Most give a full refund if you cancel 7 days before. Within 3-7 days, you lose the deposit (30-50%). Within 48 hours, you lose the full amount. To avoid losing money, choose an operator with a flexible cancellation policy or take out travel insurance that covers charter cancellations. The best months for reliable weather are May, June, and September. July and August are hot but stable, with afternoon breezes that rarely exceed 15 knots.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to rent a boat from Marbella port?
faqs.a: No, every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You can relax and enjoy the trip without any paperwork. The only exception is the licence-free day boats, which are under 6 metres and do not require a licence in Spain, but they are still operated under the skipper's supervision.

faqs.q: Which Marbella port is best for a boat rental?
faqs.a: It depends on your preference. Puerto Banús is glamorous with a party vibe, but parking is expensive and limited. Marbella Marina is quieter, cheaper, and easier to access. Both offer similar boat options, but prices may be slightly higher in Puerto Banús due to mooring fees.

faqs.q: How much does a boat rental Marbella port cost?
faqs.a: Prices vary by boat type and season. A half-day RIB costs from around EUR 500, a motor yacht from EUR 800, and a catamaran from EUR 1,300. Full-day charters are roughly double. Superyachts are price on request, starting from EUR 5,000 per day. Always check the listing for exact pricing.

faqs.q: Can I book a boat rental Marbella port for a sunset cruise?
faqs.a: Yes, many operators offer sunset cruises, usually lasting 2-3 hours in the evening. These are popular for couples and small groups. Prices start from around EUR 400 for a motor yacht. Check the search page and filter by duration to find sunset options.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a boat rental Marbella port?
faqs.a: The boating season runs from April to October. June to September is peak season with the warmest weather and water. May and October offer milder temperatures and lower prices. Winter charters are possible but less common due to cooler weather and rougher seas.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the boat?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own. Most boats have a cooler with ice. Some operators offer catering packages for an extra fee. Check the boat listing for details. Remember to bring plastic cups and avoid glass bottles for safety.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions and may cancel or postpone the trip for safety reasons. You will receive a full refund or the option to reschedule. Bad weather is rare on the Costa del Sol, but it can happen in winter or during storms.

title: Private Yacht Charter Marbella: Exclusive Experiences
metaDescription: Book a private yacht charter Marbella with a licensed skipper. Explore Puerto Banús, Costa del Sol. Compare motor yachts, catamarans from EUR 1,500. Reserve now.
h1: Private Yacht Charter Marbella: Exclusive Experiences
intro:
You want a private yacht charter in Marbella because the idea of sharing a boat with strangers sounds like a nightmare. Fair enough. A private charter means the whole vessel is yours, with a licensed skipper who knows the coast better than the back of his hand. You pick the route, the playlist, the catering. No queues, no crowds, just you and your people.

BoatHire24 connects you directly with local boat owners in Marbella, Puerto Banús, and the Costa del Sol. We list everything from 40-foot motor yachts to spacious catamarans, all available for half-day or full-day private hire. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a decent motor yacht, depending on the season and boat size. Every charter includes a skipper, fuel, and basic insurance. You just turn up and enjoy.

This page covers what you get, which ports to depart from, what boat types suit your group, and how to book without the usual hassle. If you want a private yacht charter in Marbella that actually feels exclusive, start here.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Yacht Charter Marbella Actually Includes

When you book a private yacht charter Marbella through BoatHire24, the package is straightforward. You get the boat exclusively for your group, a licensed skipper who handles navigation and mooring, fuel for the agreed itinerary, and basic insurance. Most boats also come with a Bluetooth speaker, a cooler, and basic safety equipment. Some include a paddleboard or snorkelling gear. You do not get a crew unless you book a larger vessel with a steward or chef, which is common on catamarans over 50 feet or motor yachts over 60 feet.

The skipper is not a tour guide. He will take you to the best coves and beaches if you ask, but he is there to drive the boat and keep you safe. You can bring your own food and drink, or arrange catering through the owner at an extra cost. Many owners offer a welcome drink of cava or beer, but do not assume it. Check the listing details or message the owner before you pay.

Private charters are generally for a half-day (4 hours) or a full day (8 hours). Some owners offer sunset cruises of 2-3 hours at a lower rate. Overnight charters are rare on the Costa del Sol because most boats are used for day trips, but you can find a few motor yachts with cabins for weekend hire. If you want to sleep on board, search for boats with at least 3 cabins and confirm the berth count in the listing.

Ports of Departure for Your Private Yacht Charter Marbella

Marbella has several marinas where you can start your private yacht charter. The most famous is Puerto Banús, the glitzy harbour lined with superyachts, designer shops, and beach clubs. Departing from here puts you minutes from the open sea and close to the western coves like Cala del Faro and Cala de las Tortugas. Parking is expensive and traffic is a nightmare in summer, but that is the price of glamour.

Marbella Marina, also called Puerto Deportivo, is smaller and quieter. It sits east of the old town, closer to the centre of Marbella. From here you can head east towards Cabopino or west towards Puerto Banús. It is a good option if you are staying in Marbella centre and want to avoid the Banús chaos. Parking is slightly cheaper and the marina has a few decent restaurants for a pre-charter breakfast.

Cabopino Marina, about 10 km east of Marbella, is a hidden gem. It is smaller, less crowded, and closer to the sand dunes of Artola and the nudist beach. Departing from Cabopino gives you quick access to the eastern coast and the Cala del Moral area. Estepona, 20 km west of Marbella, offers a modern marina with easy parking and a lovely old town for post-charter tapas. Sotogrande, further west, is a luxury resort with a large marina and access to the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can see dolphins on most days. Benalmádena, east of Marbella, is a busy marina with lots of boat options and a lively promenade.

Each port has its own character. If you want the full Marbella experience, go with Puerto Banús. If you prefer a relaxed vibe, choose Cabopino or Estepona. Your search on BoatHire24 lets you filter by departure port, so you can find the closest marina to your accommodation.

Boat Types for a Private Yacht Charter Marbella

Motor yachts dominate the private yacht charter Marbella scene. They are fast, stable, and offer plenty of deck space for sunbathing. A typical 40-50 foot motor yacht sleeps 6-8 people comfortably for a day trip and cruises at 20-25 knots. You can cover more distance in a half-day, reaching places like the Marbella coastline or the coves near Estepona. Prices for a half-day start from around EUR 1,500 for a 40-footer and go up to EUR 4,000 for a 60-footer with a flybridge and crew.

Catamarans are popular for groups who want stability and space. They have a wider beam, so they do not roll as much as monohulls. A 40-50 foot catamaran can carry 12-14 guests for a day trip and has a large trampoline net at the bow for lounging. They are slower than motor yachts, cruising at 8-12 knots, but the comfort is worth it. Prices start from around EUR 2,000 for a half-day. Catamarans are ideal for families with children or anyone prone to seasickness.

Sailing yachts are less common for private charters in Marbella because they require more wind and skill. But if you want the authentic sailing experience, a 40-50 foot monohull with a skipper is available. They are cheaper than motor yachts, starting from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day, but the ride is less stable and the deck space is smaller. You might enjoy the silence and the challenge of tacking, but do not expect to cover much distance.

RIBs and speedboats are for thrill-seekers. A 7-9 metre RIB with a 200-300 HP outboard can hit 40-50 knots and is perfect for a quick trip to a beach club or a water sports session. They are not suitable for large groups, max 6-8 people, and have minimal shade. Prices start from around EUR 600 for a half-day. Fishing boats are also available for private charter, usually with a skipper who knows the best spots for sea bass or bream. These are basic boats with rod holders and a fish finder, starting from EUR 800 for a half-day.

Licence-free day boats are a recent addition to the Marbella charter market. These are small motorboats, usually under 6 metres, that you can drive yourself without a licence. They are cheap, from EUR 300 for a half-day, but you are limited to calm waters and short distances. They are not really a private yacht charter, more of a DIY option. Superyachts, over 80 feet, are available for the ultra-wealthy. Prices are on request, but expect EUR 15,000-30,000 per day with full crew, champagne, and jet skis. For most people, a 40-60 foot motor yacht or catamaran is the sweet spot.

Pricing Guidance for a Private Yacht Charter Marbella

Prices for a private yacht charter Marbella vary wildly based on boat size, age, season, and duration. As a rough guide, a half-day (4 hours) on a 40-foot motor yacht in low season (November to February) starts from around EUR 1,200. In high season (June to August), the same boat costs EUR 1,800-2,500. Full-day rates are roughly double. Catamarans are 20-30% more expensive than motor yachts of the same length because of the extra space and stability.

Additional costs include VAT (21% in Spain), which is usually included in the listed price but confirm with the owner. Some owners charge a cleaning fee of EUR 50-100 if you leave the boat messy. Fuel is included in most charters, but only for a standard itinerary. If you ask the skipper to go further, like to Nerja or Gibraltar, you might pay a fuel surcharge. Food and drink are extra unless you negotiate a package. Dockage fees at the departure marina are usually included in the charter price.

You can save money by booking a half-day instead of a full day, choosing a smaller boat, or travelling in the shoulder seasons (March-May, September-October). Last-minute bookings sometimes get discounts, but do not rely on it in summer. The best deals are on sailing yachts and older motor yachts. For the latest prices, check the listings on BoatHire24 and filter by your budget.

Best Seasons for a Private Yacht Charter Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best time for a private yacht charter Marbella is from May to October, when the sea temperature is above 20 degrees C and the sun is reliable. July and August are peak season, with air temperatures hitting 35 degrees C and the water at 24-26 degrees C. The sea is calm most days, but afternoon winds can pick up, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. Book early for summer, as boats sell out weeks in advance.

April and November are shoulder months. The weather is still pleasant, 18-22 degrees C, but you risk a cloudy day or a bit of rain. The sea is cooler, around 16-18 degrees C, so swimming is possible but bracing. You will find lower prices and more availability. December to February is low season. Many boats are laid up for maintenance, and the sea can be rough due to the Atlantic swell. Some owners still offer charters, but you need to check the forecast. The advantage is empty beaches and cheap rates, around 30% less than summer.

If you want to see dolphins, the Strait of Gibraltar is best from April to September, when they follow the sardine runs. The famous orcas appear in July and August. For a sunset cruise, any month works, but the best sunsets are in October when the air is clear and the sun sets behind the mountains.

How to Book a Private Yacht Charter Marbella on BoatHire24

Booking a private yacht charter Marbella through BoatHire24 is simple. Go to the search page, enter your dates, group size, and departure port. The system shows you available boats with prices, photos, and owner profiles. You can filter by boat type, length, price range, and amenities like Wi-Fi or water toys. Each listing includes a detailed description, a calendar of availability, and a booking button.

Once you find a boat you like, click to view the full listing. Read the terms carefully: cancellation policy, included extras, skipper details. Most owners require a 30-50% deposit to confirm the booking, with the balance due 7 days before departure. Payment is handled securely through the platform. You can message the owner directly with questions before booking. We recommend asking about the itinerary, catering options, and any restrictions on music or smoking.

After you book, you will receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact information and meeting point instructions. On the day, arrive 15 minutes early at the marina. The skipper will greet you, give a safety briefing, and answer any questions. Then you are off. If you need to cancel, check the policy. Most owners offer a full refund if you cancel 14 days or more before the charter, but less than that and you lose the deposit. For high season, some owners have stricter policies.

For more tips on planning your trip, read our blog posts about Marbella yacht charters, including advice on what to pack and which coves to visit.

Who Is a Private Yacht Charter Marbella For

A private yacht charter Marbella is not for everyone. It is for people who value privacy and control. If you are a group of friends celebrating a birthday, a family reunion, or a corporate event, a private charter gives you the space to socialise without interruptions. Couples on a honeymoon or anniversary often book a private charter for a romantic sunset cruise with champagne. Business groups use private charters for networking or client entertainment in a relaxed setting.

It is also for people who hate crowded beach clubs. Instead of fighting for a sunbed at Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, you can anchor off Cala del Faro and swim in peace. The skipper will drop anchor in a quiet cove, and you can jump in, paddleboard, or just float. No queues for the bar, no loud music unless you want it.

It is not for budget travellers. A private charter costs significantly more than a shared excursion. If you are travelling solo or as a couple on a tight budget, consider a shared catamaran trip or a RIB ride. But if you have a group of 6-12 people, the per-person cost becomes reasonable. For example, a EUR 2,000 half-day charter split among 10 people is EUR 200 each, which is competitive with a decent restaurant dinner.

It is also not for people who want a party boat with a DJ and a bar. Most private charters are relaxed and family-friendly. If you want a loud party, you need to book a specific party boat or a superyacht with a crew that can handle it. Check the listing description for noise restrictions. Some owners do not allow amplified music after 6pm.

Itinerary Ideas for Your Private Yacht Charter Marbella

Your skipper will suggest a route based on the weather and your preferences, but here are three common itineraries for a private yacht charter Marbella. The Western Coves trip from Puerto Banús heads west towards Estepona. You pass the luxury villas of the Golden Mile, then the cliffs of Cala del Faro, a small cove with crystal water and a beach bar reachable only by boat. Further west, you reach the Punta de la Doncella and the Estepona marina. Total distance is about 10 NM one way. A half-day is enough for this trip with a stop for swimming and lunch.

The Eastern Coast trip from Marbella Marina or Cabopino goes east towards Calahonda and La Cala de Mijas. You can anchor off the Cabopino sand dunes or continue to the Cala del Moral, a rocky cove with clear water. If you have a full day, push on to Nerja, 30 NM east, where you can see the famous Balcón de Europa and the Nerja caves from the sea. This is a long trip, 2-3 hours each way, so you need a fast motor yacht and a full day.

The Dolphin Watching trip from Sotogrande or Estepona heads south towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The waters here are rich in plankton, attracting dolphins, whales, and sometimes orcas. The trip is about 15 NM from Estepona to the strait. You will see common dolphins and striped dolphins on most days. The skipper will stop for a swim near the Tarifa coast if conditions allow. This trip is best in the morning when the sea is calm.

For a sunset cruise, any port works. The classic route is to leave Puerto Banús at 7pm, cruise west towards the sunset, anchor off a quiet cove, and watch the sky turn orange over La Concha mountain. Return to port by 9pm. A sunset cruise is usually 2-3 hours and costs less than a half-day charter.

What to Bring on Your Private Yacht Charter Marbella

Pack light. Swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, the Andalusian sun is brutal), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket or windbreaker for the evening. If you plan to swim, bring a dry bag for your phone and wallet. Most boats have a small fridge for drinks, but bring your own water and snacks if you want specific items. The skipper will have a basic first aid kit, but bring any personal medication.

Do not bring glass bottles. Many owners ban glass on board for safety reasons. Use plastic or metal cups. If you bring food, avoid strong smells like fish or garlic, as they linger on the boat. Sandwiches, fruit, and chips are easy. Some owners provide a cooler with ice, but ask in advance.

Footwear should be non-marking white soles. Black soles leave marks on the deck. Most skippers will ask you to remove shoes on board, so bring flip-flops or go barefoot. A camera or GoPro is useful for photos, but keep it in a waterproof case. If you want music, bring a playlist on your phone. The boat's Bluetooth speaker will connect to it.

For seasickness, take a motion sickness pill an hour before departure if you are prone. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the Strait of Gibraltar can get choppy. Ginger sweets or wristbands help some people. If you are worried, choose a catamaran, which is more stable than a monohull.

Safety and Legal Considerations for a Private Yacht Charter Marbella

All boats listed on BoatHire24 are licensed and insured for commercial charter. The skipper holds a valid Professional Skipper Licence (PYC or PATRON DE YATE) and has the required safety equipment on board: life jackets, flares, fire extinguishers, and a VHF radio. The boat is inspected annually by the Spanish maritime authority. You do not need to worry about legalities, but you should know the rules.

You cannot operate the boat yourself unless you have a valid boating licence recognised in Spain. The skipper is the only person allowed to drive. Do not ask him to let you take the helm, even for a photo. It is illegal and he will refuse. Alcohol consumption is allowed on board, but the skipper must remain sober. If you get drunk and cause trouble, the skipper has the right to terminate the charter and return to port early. You will not get a refund.

Swimming is allowed only when the boat is anchored or moored, not while underway. The skipper will choose a safe spot away from other boats and jet skis. If you want to use a paddleboard or kayak, check the listing for availability. Some boats include them, others charge extra. Jet skis are not usually allowed on private charters due to insurance restrictions, but you can rent them separately at the marina.

In case of bad weather, the skipper decides whether to cancel. If the forecast shows strong winds or thunderstorms, the charter may be postponed or cancelled for safety. You will receive a full refund or a reschedule. Do not pressure the skipper to go out in dangerous conditions.

Why Book Through BoatHire24 for Your Private Yacht Charter Marbella

BoatHire24 is a marketplace, not a charter company. We do not own the boats. We connect you directly with local owners and skippers who operate on the Costa del Sol. This means you get competitive prices, a wide selection of boats, and direct communication with the person who will take you out. You can compare multiple options in one place, read reviews from previous guests, and book securely online.

Our platform covers all the major ports from Benalmádena to Sotogrande, so you can choose the most convenient departure point. We list boats in every price range, from budget RIBs to luxury superyachts. Each listing includes clear terms, photos, and a description of what is included. We do not add hidden fees. The price you see is the price you pay, plus any optional extras you agree with the owner.

If you have questions before booking, you can message the owner directly. We also have a customer support team that can help with recommendations or issues. For more information about the Marbella charter scene, read our blog posts about the best coves, local events, and seasonal tips. Start your search for a private yacht charter Marbella on BoatHire24 today.

faqs.a: Puerto Banús is the most famous and glamorous, but busy. Cabopino and Estepona are quieter and easier for parking. Choose based on your accommodation and preferred route. BoatHire24 lets you filter by departure port.

title: Boat Hire Benalmadena: Marina Rentals & Charters
metaDescription: Book boat hire Benalmadena with a licensed skipper. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs from Marina Benalmadena. Instant quotes & secure booking.
h1: Boat Hire Benalmadena: Marina Rentals & Charters
intro:
You want to get on the water from Benalmadena, but you don't own a boat and you don't have a licence. That is fine. Boat hire Benalmadena through BoatHire24 gives you a boat with a licensed skipper included, so you just turn up and enjoy. No paperwork, no navigation stress, just the Costa del Sol sun and a cold drink.

Benalmadena Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is one of the most convenient departure points on the coast, 10 minutes from Malaga Airport and right next to the town centre. You can be sipping a beer on a motor yacht 15 minutes after parking your car. The marina has plenty of restaurants, a casino, and the Sea Life aquarium if you want to make a day of it before or after your charter.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Benalmadena Boat Hire

Every booking through BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper who holds a Spanish licence and speaks English. The skipper handles the navigation, docking, and safety briefings. You do not need any boating experience or a licence. Fuel is typically included in the price for a standard day charter (8 hours), but always check the listing details because some high-performance RIBs or long-distance trips may charge fuel separately. Most boats include a cooler with ice, Bluetooth speakers, and a sun deck. Some larger yachts have a full kitchen, BBQ, and even a jet ski to tow.

You can bring your own food and drink, or order a catering package through the boat owner. The skipper will recommend a good spot for swimming, anchor at a calm cove, and give you space to relax. You are not expected to help with anything unless you want to.

Departure Port: Benalmadena Marina (Puerto Deportivo)

Benalmadena Marina is a modern, well-sheltered port with around 900 berths and a lively promenade. It is less glitzy than Puerto Banus but more authentic, with a mix of fishing boats, small yachts, and charter vessels. The marina has free WiFi, clean showers, and plenty of parking (paid parking nearby, around EUR 2 per hour). You can walk from the marina to the Benalmadena cable car (Teleferico) and the Stupa Buddhist temple in about 20 minutes.

From the marina, you can head east towards Malaga city and the Nerja caves (about 2 hours by boat), or west towards Cabopino, Puerto Banus, and Estepona. The most popular route is west, hugging the coast past the sand dunes of Cabopino and the exclusive villas of Puerto Banus. You will see La Concha mountain in the background the whole time.

Boat Options Available for Hire in Benalmadena

You can hire motor yachts from 6 metres (RIB speedboats) up to 20 metres (luxury motor yachts with cabins). Catamarans are popular for groups because they have more deck space and stability. Sailing yachts are available if you want a quieter, wind-powered experience. Fishing boats with rod holders and fish finders are also listed for anglers.

For small groups (2-6 people), a RIB or a 7-metre motor boat is ideal. Prices start from around EUR 350 for a half day (4 hours). For larger groups (8-12 people), a 10-12 metre motor yacht or catamaran costs from around EUR 800 for a full day. Superyachts (15 metres plus) are available for VIP experiences, with prices on request.

You can browse all options on the search page and filter by boat type, price, and availability.

Pricing Guidance for Benalmadena Boat Hire

Prices vary by boat size, age, and season. Here is a rough guide based on typical listings:

  • RIB / speedboat (6-7m): half day from EUR 350, full day from EUR 550
  • Motor yacht (8-10m): half day from EUR 500, full day from EUR 800
  • Catamaran (10-12m): half day from EUR 600, full day from EUR 1,000
  • Sailing yacht (10-12m): half day from EUR 450, full day from EUR 750
  • Luxury motor yacht (15-20m): full day from EUR 2,500, price on request

All prices include skipper and fuel for a standard day charter (8 hours). Half days are usually 4 hours, morning or afternoon. You can also book multi-day charters with overnight stays in a different port, but that costs extra.

Best Season for Boat Hire in Benalmadena

The season runs from April to October, with peak months July and August. In summer, daytime temperatures hit 30-35 degrees C, and the sea is calm in the morning. Afternoon winds pick up, so morning charters are smoother. Spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) are cooler (22-28 degrees C) and less crowded, with lower prices and better availability.

Winter charters (November-March) are possible but less common. The sea can be rough, and many boats are laid up for maintenance. If you want a winter charter, check the listing carefully and expect higher wind speeds.

How to Book a Boat in Benalmadena

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You go to the BoatHire24 homepage, enter Benalmadena as the port, select your date and group size, and browse the available boats. Each listing shows photos, a description, the price, and the cancellation policy. You can send a direct message to the owner to ask questions before booking. Payment is processed securely online, and you receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact details.

You should book at least 48 hours in advance, especially in summer. Last-minute bookings are sometimes possible, but availability is limited. If you need a specific boat type or a large group, book a week ahead.

Who Is Boat Hire Benalmadena For

Boat hire in Benalmadena works for families with children (most boats have safety rails and life jackets), couples looking for a romantic sunset cruise, groups of friends wanting a party boat, and corporate teams for team-building days. It is also good for photographers who want to shoot the coastline from the sea. The skipper can take you to the best spots for dolphin watching near the Strait of Gibraltar, or to the Cala del Faro cove for swimming.

If you are staying in Torremolinos, Fuengirola, or Mijas, Benalmadena marina is a short taxi ride away. You can also charter a boat from Benalmadena to visit Puerto Banus for lunch and then return in the afternoon. The distance is about 12 nautical miles, so it takes around 45 minutes at cruising speed.

Local Attractions Near Benalmadena Marina

Before or after your charter, you can explore the Benalmadena Marina area. The marina has a casino, several seafood restaurants, and a small beach (Playa de la Marina). The nearby Benalmadena cable car takes you to the top of Mount Calamorro for panoramic views. The Stupa Buddhist temple is a 15-minute walk uphill and offers a peaceful garden. For families, the Sea Life aquarium and the Tivoli World amusement park are within 5 minutes by car.

If you want to combine your boat hire with a visit to the Nerja caves, you can charter a boat east from Benalmadena. The journey takes about 2 hours each way, so you need a full day charter. The skipper can anchor near the cliffs below the caves, but you cannot access the caves from the sea directly. You would need to dock at Nerja port and take a taxi to the cave entrance.

For more tips on the best routes and what to pack, read the BoatHire24 blog.

Safety and Legal Requirements

All boats on BoatHire24 are insured and have valid safety equipment (life jackets, flares, fire extinguishers, first aid kit). The skipper holds a Spanish professional licence and is responsible for the safety of the vessel and passengers. You do not sign any liability waivers; the charter contract covers the owner and the skipper. Children must wear life jackets while on deck, and the skipper will provide age-appropriate sizes.

If you have any medical conditions or mobility issues, inform the owner before booking. Most boats are not wheelchair accessible, but some larger catamarans have a flat deck and a boarding ramp. Ask the owner directly.

Summary: Why Choose Boat Hire Benalmadena

Benalmadena is a practical, affordable base for a boat charter on the Costa del Sol. The marina is easy to reach, the boats are well-maintained, and the coastline offers varied scenery from sandy beaches to rocky coves. You get a skipper included, so you can relax and enjoy the day without any hassle. Whether you want a fast RIB for a thrill or a spacious catamaran for a party, BoatHire24 has options to suit your group size and budget.

Choosing the Right Boat for Your Benalmadena Group

If you are a group of four or fewer, a RIB or a small motor boat gives you speed and agility. You can zip from Benalmadena to the Cala del Faro in 15 minutes, anchor for a swim, and be back in time for lunch. For six to ten people, a catamaran is the better bet. The wide deck means nobody is squeezing past each other, and the trampoline net at the bow is a sunbathing spot that beats any beach. Groups of twelve or more should look at a motor yacht with a flybridge. That upper deck gives you a second social area, and the skipper can stay at the helm while you party below. Every boat type has its trade-off, so think about what your group actually wants to do. If the plan is to drink and dance, you need space, not speed. If you want to explore as many coves as possible, a RIB is your friend.

What to Expect from the Benalmadena Coastline

The coast west of Benalmadena is a mix of built-up resorts and quiet stretches. You leave the marina and pass the Benalmadena beachfront, then you hit the Torre de Benal area with its small beach bars. Further west, the coast opens up at Cabopino, where the sand dunes are protected and the water is clear. You can anchor there and swim to the shore for a walk through the Artola dunes. Continuing west, you see the luxury villas of Elviria and then Puerto Banus. The whole stretch from Benalmadena to Puerto Banus is about 12 nautical miles, so you can do it in under an hour at cruising speed. East of Benalmadena, the coast is less developed. You pass the fishing village of La Cala del Moral and then Malaga city. If you have a full day, you can go all the way to the Nerja caves, but that is a two-hour run each way. The skipper will know the best anchorages based on the wind direction and sea state that day.

Combining Benalmadena with a Puerto Banus Stop

A popular itinerary is to leave Benalmadena in the morning, cruise west to Puerto Banus for a lunch stop, then return in the afternoon. You can dock at the Puerto Banus marina for a few hours, but you need to book a berth in advance through the charter owner. The cost for a few hours at the Puerto Banus marina is around EUR 50-100 depending on the boat length. Once there, you can walk to the designer shops, eat at a beachfront restaurant, or just watch the superyachts. Then you head back to Benalmadena for a late afternoon swim. This works well because you get the Benalmadena departure convenience with the Puerto Banus glamour. The skipper will handle the docking and paperwork, so you just step off and enjoy.

Sunset Charters from Benalmadena

Sunset charters are a separate product from day charters. They typically run from 18:00 to 21:00 in summer, or 16:00 to 19:00 in spring and autumn. The price is lower than a full day, usually around 60-70 percent of the half-day rate. You get the same boat and skipper, but the focus is on the evening atmosphere. The sun sets behind La Concha mountain, and the sea turns orange and pink. Most sunset charters include a glass of cava or a soft drink, but you can bring your own. The skipper will anchor at a spot with a clear view of the horizon. Sunset charters are popular for proposals, birthday celebrations, or just a quiet evening. You can book them through the same search process on BoatHire24, just filter for "sunset" or contact the owner directly.

Fishing Charters from Benalmadena

If fishing is your aim, Benalmadena has dedicated fishing boats with rod holders, bait, and fish finders. These are different from the standard leisure boats. The skipper knows the local fishing grounds, including the reefs off Cabopino and the deeper waters near the Strait of Gibraltar. You can target species like sea bass, bream, mackerel, and sometimes tuna. Fishing charters are usually half-day trips (4 hours) and cost from around EUR 400 for a small boat. The skipper provides all the tackle and handles the catch. You can keep what you catch or release it. Fishing licences are not required for recreational fishing on a charter boat in Spain, as the skipper holds a commercial licence that covers the passengers. Check the listing to confirm that the boat is equipped for fishing, not just a general boat that allows fishing.

Booking for Special Occasions in Benalmadena

Boat hire in Benalmadena works well for birthdays, anniversaries, and stag or hen parties. For a birthday, you can book a catamaran with a catering package that includes a cake and decorations. The skipper will help you set up a playlist on the Bluetooth speakers. For stag or hen groups, the skipper is fine with drinking and music as long as guests do not jump off the boat in dangerous areas. Some owners charge a cleaning deposit for large groups, usually EUR 200-300, refundable if the boat is returned clean. Corporate events are also possible, with boats that have a presentation screen or a quiet cabin for meetings. You can request a specific itinerary, such as a team-building swim stop or a lunch at a beach club. Always mention the occasion when you book, so the owner can prepare the boat accordingly.

What to Bring on Your Benalmadena Boat Charter

You need sunscreen with at least SPF 50, a hat, and sunglasses. The sun reflects off the water and burns quickly. Bring a towel and a change of clothes if you plan to swim. A light jacket or windbreaker is useful for the afternoon breeze. You can bring your own food and drink, but keep it in a soft cooler bag, not a hard one that takes up deck space. Glass bottles are not allowed on most boats because broken glass on deck is dangerous. Use cans or plastic bottles. If you forget anything, the marina has a supermarket and a few shops. The skipper will have water on board, but bring extra if you are a heavy drinker. For electronics, bring a waterproof phone pouch or keep your phone in a dry bag. The boat has USB ports for charging, but do not rely on them.

Accessing Benalmadena Marina by Public Transport

Benalmadena Marina is easy to reach without a car. The nearest train station is Benalmadena-Arroyo de la Miel, which is on the Cercanias line from Malaga and Fuengirola. From the station, you take bus line 112 or a taxi (about EUR 8). The bus stop is at the marina entrance. If you are staying in Torremolinos, the bus line 110 runs directly to the marina. From Malaga airport, you can take the train to Benalmadena-Arroyo de la Miel and then a taxi. The whole journey from the airport takes about 40 minutes. Parking at the marina is paid and limited, so public transport is often easier. The marina has a taxi rank at the main entrance, so getting back after the charter is straightforward.

Weather and Sea Conditions for Benalmadena Charters

The Mediterranean off Benalmadena is generally calm, but the wind can pick up in the afternoon. The typical pattern is a light breeze in the morning (5-10 knots) that builds to 15-20 knots by 14:00. This is called the "poniente" wind and comes from the west. The sea state stays moderate, with waves of 0.5 to 1 metre. In summer, the sea is flatter because the thermal winds are weaker. In spring and autumn, you can get stronger winds from the east (levante), which can make the sea choppy. The skipper will check the forecast and may recommend a morning charter if the wind is expected to rise. If the sea is too rough, the charter can be rescheduled with no penalty, as safety comes first. Always check the weather forecast on the day, but trust the skipper's judgement.

Insurance and Cancellation Policies

Each boat listing on BoatHire24 shows the cancellation policy. The most common is a full refund if you cancel 72 hours before the charter. Some owners offer a 48-hour window, but you may lose 50 percent. In case of bad weather, the owner or skipper will cancel and you get a full refund or a free reschedule. The boat insurance covers third-party liability and damage to the vessel, but it does not cover your personal belongings. Keep your valuables in a waterproof bag and do not leave them on deck unattended. If you cause damage through reckless behaviour, you may be liable, but this is rare. The skipper will give a safety briefing at the start and will stop any unsafe activity. Read the cancellation policy before you book, and if you are unsure, contact the owner directly.

Multi-Day Charters from Benalmadena

If you want to explore further, you can book a multi-day charter. This means you sleep on the boat overnight and move to a different port each day. From Benalmadena, a common route is to go west to Estepona for the first night, then to Gibraltar for the second day, and back to Benalmadena on the third day. The skipper stays on board and handles the navigation. You need to pay for marina berths overnight, which cost around EUR 50-150 per night depending on the port. The boat must have cabins and a galley. Most motor yachts over 12 metres have at least two cabins and a small kitchen. Catamarans often have four cabins. Multi-day charters are priced per day plus the overnight berth fees. Contact the owner for a custom quote, as these are not always listed on the search page.

For more detailed advice on planning your trip, read the BoatHire24 blog.
